<content>
1: # Session B - Backend Tasks
2: 
3: ## Owner
4: 
5: - Backend Engineer (Go API + SQLite)
6: 
7: ## Objective
8: 
9: - Deliver stable API and data layer for auth, profile, pantry, meal plans, macros, and consumption flows.
10: 
11: ## Ordered Task List
12: 
13: ### 1) Schema and setup first (0:30-1:30)
14: 
15: - [ ] `P0` Implement SQLite schema + migrations for:
16:   - users
17:   - user_body_metrics
18:   - user_preferences
19:   - budgets
20:   - purchases
21:   - ingredients
22:   - recipes
23:   - recipe_ingredients
24:   - pantry_items
25:   - meal_plans
26:   - plan_meals
27:   - consumption_log
28:   - chat_messages
29:   - favorites (optional table behind feature flag/time)
30: - [ ] `P0` Add one-command local DB setup/reset path.
31: - [ ] `P0` Seed ingredients and minimal recipes for deterministic demo.
32: 
33: ### 2) Auth and profile APIs (1:30-2:30)
34: 
35: - [ ] `P0` Auth endpoints: `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`, `GET /me`.
36: - [ ] `P0` Password hashing and token/session expiry rules.
37: - [ ] `P0` Profile endpoints for metrics, preferences, budget:
38:   - `GET /profile`
39:   - `PATCH /profile/metrics`
40:   - `PATCH /profile/preferences`
41:   - `PATCH /profile/budget`
42: - [ ] `P0` Publish API v1 examples for frontend and AI sessions.
43: 
44: ### 3) Pantry and recipes APIs (2:30-3:30)
45: 
46: - [ ] `P0` `GET /ingredients/search?q=` for pantry add flow.
47: - [ ] `P0` `POST /pantry/items` (ingredient + quantity + unit).
48: - [ ] `P0` `PATCH /pantry/items/:id` (increment/decrement quantity).
49: - [ ] `P0` `DELETE /pantry/items/:id`.
50: - [ ] `P0` `GET /recipes/:id` with ingredient quantities and macros.
51: - [ ] `P1` Purchases endpoints for budget tracking:
52:   - `POST /purchases`
53:   - `GET /budget/summary`
54: 
55: ### 4) Plan persistence and macro aggregation (3:30-5:00)
56: 
57: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/proposal` ingestion endpoint for AI-normalized payload.
58: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/accept` persists to meal plans.
59: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/decline` archives/rejects and supports regeneration flow.
60: - [ ] `P0` `GET /plans/week?start=` returns 7-day calendar payload.
61: - [ ] `P0` Macro totals service in responses:
62:   - per meal
63:   - per day
64:   - per week
65: 
66: ### 5) Consumption and pantry deduction (5:00-6:00)
67: 
68: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plan-meals/:id/consume` marks consumed with timestamp.
69: - [ ] `P0` Deduct pantry ingredients by recipe quantities.
70: - [ ] `P0` Prevent negative pantry values (clamp at zero + warning field).
71: - [ ] `P0` Log deductions to `consumption_log` for audit/debug.
72: 
73: ### 6) Hardening for demo (6:00-7:00)
74: 
75: - [ ] `P0` Add consistent error format for frontend handling.
76: - [ ] `P0` Add lightweight request logging and key action tracing.
77: - [ ] `P0` Add smoke checks for auth -> plan accept -> consume flow.
78: - [ ] `P1` Add favorite proposal endpoint if time remains.
79: 
80: ## Contracts Needed From Others
81: 
82: - PM: frozen priority and final scope boundaries.
83: - AI: normalized proposal JSON schema and ingredient matching policy.
84: - Frontend: required response fields per screen.
85: - QA: top-priority test scenarios and failure expectations.
86: 
87: ## Risks
88: 
89: - AI names not matching ingredient table.
90: - Unit mismatches (g/ml/piece) creating deduction inaccuracies.
91: - Time overrun from building too many endpoints before core path is stable.
92: 
93: ## Done Criteria
94: 
95: - API supports full happy path from auth to pantry auto-deduction.
96: - Weekly plan endpoint returns 7-day, 4-section layout with macro totals.
97: - DB setup/seed is reproducible in one command.
98: - Frontend can run without backend contract guesswork.

(End of file - total 98 lines)
</content>

1: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
2: 
3: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
4:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
5:     email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
6:     password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
7:     display_name TEXT,
8:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
9:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
10: );
11: 
12: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_body_metrics (
13:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
14:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
15:     height_cm REAL,
16:     weight_kg REAL,
17:     age INTEGER,
18:     sex TEXT,
19:     activity_level TEXT,
20:     goal TEXT,
21:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
22:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
23:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
24:     CHECK (sex IN ('female', 'male', 'other', 'prefer_not_to_say') OR sex IS NULL)
25: );
26: 
27: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS user_preferences (
28:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
29:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
30:     diet_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'omnivore',
31:     allergies_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
32:     dislikes_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
33:     likes_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
34:     daily_calorie_target INTEGER,
35:     notes TEXT,
36:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
37:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
38:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
39: );
40: 
41: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS budgets (
42:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
43:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
44:     month TEXT NOT NULL,
45:     currency TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD',
46:     amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (amount_cents >= 0),
47:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
48:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
49:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
50:     UNIQUE (user_id, month)
51: );
52: 
53: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ingredients (
54:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
55:     name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
56:     canonical_unit TEXT NOT NULL,
57:     kcal_per_unit REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
58:     protein_g_per_unit REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
59:     carbs_g_per_unit REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
60:     fat_g_per_unit REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
61:     estimated_cost_cents_per_unit INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
62:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
63:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
64: );
65: 
66: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS purchases (
67:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
68:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
69:     budget_id TEXT,
70:     ingredient_id TEXT,
71:     description TEXT,
72:     quantity REAL,
73:     unit TEXT,
74:     cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (cost_cents >= 0),
75:     purchased_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
76:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
77:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
78:     FOREIGN KEY (budget_id) REFERENCES budgets(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
79:     FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES ingredients(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
80: );
81: 
82: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipes (
83:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
84:     name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
85:     meal_type TEXT NOT NULL,
86:     servings INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (servings > 0),
87:     instructions TEXT,
88:     total_kcal REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
89:     total_protein_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
90:     total_carbs_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
91:     total_fat_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
92:     estimated_cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
93:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
94:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
95:     CHECK (meal_type IN ('breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner', 'snacks'))
96: );
97: 
98: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipe_ingredients (
99:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
100:     recipe_id TEXT NOT NULL,
101:     ingredient_id TEXT NOT NULL,
102:     quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity >= 0),
103:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
104:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
105:     FOREIGN KEY (recipe_id) REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
106:     FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES ingredients(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
107:     UNIQUE (recipe_id, ingredient_id, unit)
108: );
109: 
110: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pantry_items (
111:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
112:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
113:     ingredient_id TEXT NOT NULL,
114:     quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity >= 0),
115:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
116:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
117:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
118:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
119:     FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES ingredients(id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
120:     UNIQUE (user_id, ingredient_id, unit)
121: );
122: 
123: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meal_plans (
124:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
125:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
126:     period_type TEXT NOT NULL,
127:     start_date DATE NOT NULL,
128:     end_date DATE NOT NULL,
129:     status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'accepted',
130:     source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ai',
131:     proposal_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
132:     ai_cost_cents_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (ai_cost_cents_total >= 0),
133:     notes TEXT,
134:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
135:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
136:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
137:     CHECK (period_type IN ('day', 'week', 'month')),
138:     CHECK (status IN ('proposal', 'accepted', 'declined', 'archived')),
139:     CHECK (source IN ('ai', 'fallback', 'manual')),
140:     CHECK (start_date <= end_date)
141: );
142: 
143: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_meals (
144:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
145:     meal_plan_id TEXT NOT NULL,
146:     recipe_id TEXT,
147:     scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL,
148:     meal_section TEXT NOT NULL,
149:     recipe_name TEXT NOT NULL,
150:     servings REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (servings > 0),
151:     kcal REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
152:     protein_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
153:     carbs_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
154:     fat_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
155:     estimated_cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
156:     is_consumed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (is_consumed IN (0, 1)),
157:     consumed_at DATETIME,
158:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
159:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
160:     FOREIGN KEY (meal_plan_id) REFERENCES meal_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
161:     FOREIGN KEY (recipe_id) REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
162:     CHECK (meal_section IN ('breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner', 'snacks')),
163:     UNIQUE (meal_plan_id, scheduled_date, meal_section)
164: );
165: 
166: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS consumption_log (
167:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
168:     plan_meal_id TEXT,
169:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
170:     ingredient_id TEXT,
171:     pantry_item_id TEXT,
172:     quantity_deducted REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity_deducted >= 0),
173:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
174:     before_quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (before_quantity >= 0),
175:     after_quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (after_quantity >= 0),
176:     warning TEXT,
177:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
178:     FOREIGN KEY (plan_meal_id) REFERENCES plan_meals(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
179:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
180:     FOREIGN KEY (ingredient_id) REFERENCES ingredients(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
181:     FOREIGN KEY (pantry_item_id) REFERENCES pantry_items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
182: );
183: 
184: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
185:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
186:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
187:     meal_plan_id TEXT,
188:     role TEXT NOT NULL,
189:     action TEXT,
190:     content TEXT NOT NULL,
191:     metadata_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
192:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
193:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
194:     FOREIGN KEY (meal_plan_id) REFERENCES meal_plans(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
195:     CHECK (role IN ('system', 'user', 'assistant', 'tool'))
196: );
197: 
198: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_email ON users(email);
199: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ingredients_name ON ingredients(name);
200: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pantry_items_user_id ON pantry_items(user_id);
201: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pantry_items_ingredient_id ON pantry_items(ingredient_id);
202: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_meal_plans_user_period ON meal_plans(user_id, period_type, start_date);
203: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_plan_meals_meal_plan_id ON plan_meals(meal_plan_id);
204: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_plan_meals_schedule ON plan_meals(scheduled_date, meal_section);
205: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_consumption_log_user_created_at ON consumption_log(user_id, created_at);
206: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_chat_messages_user_created_at ON chat_messages(user_id, created_at);

(End of file - total 206 lines)
</content>

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: 
3: set -euo pipefail
4: 
5: ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
6: DB_PATH="${DB_PATH:-$ROOT_DIR/database/sqlite/pantrypal.db}"
7: MIGRATION_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/migrations/001_init_schema.sql"
8: SEED_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql"
9: MODE="${1:-reset}"
10: 
11: if [[ ! -f "$MIGRATION_PATH" ]]; then
12:   printf "Missing migration file: %s\n" "$MIGRATION_PATH" >&2
13:   exit 1
14: fi
15: 
16: if [[ ! -f "$SEED_PATH" ]]; then
17:   printf "Missing seed file: %s\n" "$SEED_PATH" >&2
18:   exit 1
19: fi
20: 
21: mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DB_PATH")"
22: 
23: case "$MODE" in
24:   setup)
25:     if [[ -f "$DB_PATH" ]]; then
26:       printf "Database already exists: %s\n" "$DB_PATH"
27:       printf "Use '%s reset' to recreate it.\n" "$0"
28:       exit 0
29:     fi
30:     ;;
31:   reset)
32:     rm -f "$DB_PATH"
33:     ;;
34:   *)
35:     printf "Usage: %s [setup|reset]\n" "$0" >&2
36:     exit 1
37:     ;;
38: esac
39: 
40: sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" < "$MIGRATION_PATH"
41: sqlite3 "$DB_PATH" < "$SEED_PATH"
42:

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

ROOT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
DB_PATH="${DB_PATH:-$ROOT_DIR/database/sqlite/pantrypal.db}"
MIGRATION_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/migrations/001_init_schema.sql"
SEED_PATH="$ROOT_DIR/backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql"
MODE="${1:-reset}"

if [[ ! -f "$MIGRATION_PATH" ]]; then
  printf "Missing migration file: %s\n" "$MIGRATION_PATH" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [[ ! -f "$SEED_PATH" ]]; then
  printf "Missing seed file: %s\n" "$SEED_PATH" >&2
  exit 1
fi

Local SQLite setup for backend Phase 1 tasks.

## One-command reset

Run from repo root:

```bash
./scripts/db/reset-local-db
```

This will:
- Recreate `database/sqlite/pantrypal.db`
- Apply `backend/migrations/001_init_schema.sql`
- Seed demo data from `backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql`

## Optional commands

Create DB only if missing:

parts.text:
Task 1 looks good overall — I’d mark it **PASS** for backend QA.

- **Schema + migrations implemented:** `backend/migrations/001_init_schema.sql:1` creates all required P0 tables from task 1 (`users` through `chat_messages`), and I verified they exist in SQLite after reset.
- **Optional table:** `favorites` is not present; since it is explicitly optional in `tasks/backend.md:29`, this is acceptable for now.
- **One-command local DB reset path:** `scripts/db/reset-local-db:1` works and calls `scripts/db/local_db.sh:1`; running it successfully recreates the DB and applies migration + seed.
- **Setup/reset behavior is correct:** `scripts/db/local_db.sh:23` supports `setup|reset`, with `setup` safely no-op’ing when DB exists and `reset` recreating deterministically.
- **Deterministic demo seed present:** `backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql:1` seeds a fixed demo user, ingredients, recipes, recipe_ingredients, and pantry_items with stable IDs/upsert logic.
- **Verification run results:** after reset, key seeded counts are stable (`ingredients=10`, `recipes=4`, `pantry_items=10`) and tables list matches expected scope.

Minor note:
- If you want stricter migration hygiene later, consider adding migration numbering/tooling beyond a single SQL file, but for Task 1 scope this implementation is valid.

21: func (h *AuthHandler) Register(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
22: 	var req dto.RegisterRequest
23: 	if err := ReadJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
24: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "bad_request", "invalid JSON payload")
25: 		return
26: 	}
27: 
28: 	res, err := h.auth.Register(r.Context(), req)
29: 	if err != nil {
30: 		switch {
31: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidEmail), errors.Is(err, services.ErrWeakPassword):
32: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "validation_error", err.Error())
33: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrEmailConflict):
34: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusConflict, "conflict", err.Error())
35: 		default:
36: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to register user")
37: 		}
38: 		return
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusCreated, res)
42: }
43: 
44: func (h *AuthHandler) Login(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
45: 	var req dto.LoginRequest
46: 	if err := ReadJSON(r, &req); err != nil {
47: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "bad_request", "invalid JSON payload")
48: 		return
49: 	}
50: 
51: 	res, err := h.auth.Login(r.Context(), req)
52: 	if err != nil {
53: 		switch {
54: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidCredentials):
55: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", err.Error())
56: 		default:
57: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to login")
58: 		}
59: 		return
60: 	}
61: 
62: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, res)
63: }
64: 
65: func (h *AuthHandler) Me(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
66: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
67: 	if !ok {
68: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")
69: 		return
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	me, err := h.auth.Me(r.Context(), userID)
73: 	if err != nil {
74: 		if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
75: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "user not found")
76: 			return
77: 		}
78: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to load current user")
79: 		return
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, map[string]dto.UserResponse{"user": me})
83: }

1: package services
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"errors"
7: 	"strings"
8: 
9: 	"golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt"
10: 
11: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/auth"
12: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
13: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
14: )
15: 
16: var (
17: 	ErrInvalidEmail       = errors.New("email is invalid")
18: 	ErrWeakPassword       = errors.New("password must be at least 8 characters")
19: 	ErrEmailConflict      = errors.New("email already registered")
20: 	ErrInvalidCredentials = errors.New("invalid credentials")
21: )
22: 
23: type AuthService struct {
24: 	users  *repositories.UserRepository
25: 	tokens *auth.TokenManager
26: }
27: 
28: func NewAuthService(users *repositories.UserRepository, tokens *auth.TokenManager) *AuthService {
29: 	return &AuthService{users: users, tokens: tokens}
30: }
31: 
32: func (s *AuthService) Register(ctx context.Context, req dto.RegisterRequest) (dto.AuthResponse, error) {
33: 	req.Email = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Email))
34: 	req.DisplayName = strings.TrimSpace(req.DisplayName)
35: 	if !strings.Contains(req.Email, "@") {
36: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrInvalidEmail
37: 	}
38: 	if len(req.Password) < 8 {
39: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrWeakPassword
40: 	}
41: 
42: 	exists, err := s.users.ExistsByEmail(ctx, req.Email)
43: 	if err != nil {
44: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
45: 	}
46: 	if exists {
47: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrEmailConflict
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	hash, err := bcrypt.GenerateFromPassword([]byte(req.Password), bcrypt.DefaultCost)
51: 	if err != nil {
52: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	user, err := s.users.CreateUser(ctx, req.Email, string(hash), req.DisplayName)
56: 	if err != nil {
57: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	token, expiresAt, err := s.tokens.MakeToken(user.ID)
61: 	if err != nil {
62: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	return dto.AuthResponse{
66: 		Token:     token,
67: 		ExpiresAt: expiresAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00"),
68: 		User: dto.UserResponse{
69: 			ID:          user.ID,
70: 			Email:       user.Email,
71: 			DisplayName: user.DisplayName,
72: 		},
73: 	}, nil
74: }
75: 
76: func (s *AuthService) Login(ctx context.Context, req dto.LoginRequest) (dto.AuthResponse, error) {
77: 	req.Email = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(req.Email))
78: 	user, err := s.users.GetByEmail(ctx, req.Email)
79: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
80: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrInvalidCredentials
81: 	}
82: 	if err != nil {
83: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
84: 	}
85: 	if bcrypt.CompareHashAndPassword([]byte(user.PasswordHash), []byte(req.Password)) != nil {
86: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, ErrInvalidCredentials
87: 	}
88: 
89: 	if err := s.users.EnsureDefaultProfileRows(ctx, user.ID); err != nil {
90: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	token, expiresAt, err := s.tokens.MakeToken(user.ID)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return dto.AuthResponse{}, err
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	return dto.AuthResponse{
99: 		Token:     token,
100: 		ExpiresAt: expiresAt.Format("2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00"),
101: 		User: dto.UserResponse{
102: 			ID:          user.ID,
103: 			Email:       user.Email,
104: 			DisplayName: user.DisplayName,
105: 		},
106: 	}, nil
107: }
108: 
109: func (s *AuthService) Me(ctx context.Context, userID string) (dto.UserResponse, error) {
110: 	user, err := s.users.GetByID(ctx, userID)
111: 	if err != nil {
112: 		return dto.UserResponse{}, err
113: 	}
114: 	return dto.UserResponse{ID: user.ID, Email: user.Email, DisplayName: user.DisplayName}, nil
115: }

(End of file - total 115 lines)
</content>

1: package repositories
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"encoding/json"
7: 	"errors"
8: 	"strings"
9: 	"time"
10: 
11: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/id"
12: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
13: )
14: 
15: type User struct {
16: 	ID           string
17: 	Email        string
18: 	PasswordHash string
19: 	DisplayName  string
20: }
21: 
22: type UserRepository struct {
23: 	db *sql.DB
24: }
25: 
26: func NewUserRepository(db *sql.DB) *UserRepository {
27: 	return &UserRepository{db: db}
28: }
29: 
30: func (r *UserRepository) ExistsByEmail(ctx context.Context, email string) (bool, error) {
31: 	var n int
32: 	if err := r.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(1) FROM users WHERE email = ?`, email).Scan(&n); err != nil {
33: 		return false, err
34: 	}
35: 	return n > 0, nil
36: }
37: 
38: func (r *UserRepository) CreateUser(ctx context.Context, email, passwordHash, displayName string) (User, error) {
39: 	userID, err := id.New("usr")
40: 	if err != nil {
41: 		return User{}, err
42: 	}
43: 
44: 	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
45: 		ctx,
46: 		`INSERT INTO users (id, email, password_hash, display_name) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)`,
47: 		userID,
48: 		email,
49: 		passwordHash,
50: 		displayName,
51: 	)
52: 	if err != nil {
53: 		return User{}, err
54: 	}
55: 
56: 	if err := r.EnsureDefaultProfileRows(ctx, userID); err != nil {
57: 		return User{}, err
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	return User{ID: userID, Email: email, DisplayName: displayName}, nil
61: }
62: 
63: func (r *UserRepository) GetByEmail(ctx context.Context, email string) (User, error) {
64: 	var out User
65: 	var displayName sql.NullString
66: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
67: 		ctx,
68: 		`SELECT id, email, password_hash, display_name FROM users WHERE email = ?`,
69: 		email,
70: 	).Scan(&out.ID, &out.Email, &out.PasswordHash, &displayName)
71: 	if err != nil {
72: 		return User{}, err
73: 	}
74: 	if displayName.Valid {
75: 		out.DisplayName = displayName.String
76: 	}
77: 	return out, nil
78: }
79: 
80: func (r *UserRepository) GetByID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (User, error) {
81: 	var out User
82: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
83: 		ctx,
84: 		`SELECT id, email, COALESCE(display_name, '') FROM users WHERE id = ?`,
85: 		userID,
86: 	).Scan(&out.ID, &out.Email, &out.DisplayName)
87: 	if err != nil {
88: 		return User{}, err
89: 	}
90: 	return out, nil
91: }
92: 
93: func (r *UserRepository) ExistsByID(ctx context.Context, userID string) (bool, error) {
94: 	var n int
95: 	if err := r.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(1) FROM users WHERE id = ?`, userID).Scan(&n); err != nil {
96: 		return false, err
97: 	}
98: 	return n > 0, nil
99: }
100: 
101: func (r *UserRepository) EnsureDefaultProfileRows(ctx context.Context, userID string) error {
102: 	if _, err := r.db.ExecContext(
103: 		ctx,
104: 		`INSERT INTO user_preferences (id, user_id, diet_type, allergies_json, dislikes_json, likes_json)
105:          VALUES (?, ?, 'omnivore', '[]', '[]', '[]')
106:          ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO NOTHING`,
107: 		id.Must("upr"),
108: 		userID,
109: 	); err != nil {
110: 		return err
111: 	}
112: 	if _, err := r.db.ExecContext(
113: 		ctx,
114: 		`INSERT INTO user_body_metrics (id, user_id)
115:          VALUES (?, ?)
116:          ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO NOTHING`,
117: 		id.Must("ubm"),
118: 		userID,
119: 	); err != nil {
120: 		return err
121: 	}
122: 	return nil
123: }
124: 
125: func (r *UserRepository) GetProfile(ctx context.Context, userID string) (dto.ProfileResponse, error) {
126: 	if err := r.EnsureDefaultProfileRows(ctx, userID); err != nil {
127: 		return dto.ProfileResponse{}, err
128: 	}
129: 
130: 	user, err := r.GetByID(ctx, userID)
131: 	if err != nil {
132: 		return dto.ProfileResponse{}, err
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	out := dto.ProfileResponse{
136: 		User: dto.UserResponse{
137: 			ID:          user.ID,
138: 			Email:       user.Email,
139: 			DisplayName: user.DisplayName,
140: 		},
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	var sex sql.NullString
144: 	var activity sql.NullString
145: 	var goal sql.NullString
146: 	err = r.db.QueryRowContext(
147: 		ctx,
148: 		`SELECT height_cm, weight_kg, age, sex, activity_level, goal FROM user_body_metrics WHERE user_id = ?`,
149: 		userID,
150: 	).Scan(&out.Metrics.HeightCM, &out.Metrics.WeightKG, &out.Metrics.Age, &sex, &activity, &goal)
151: 	if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
152: 		return dto.ProfileResponse{}, err
153: 	}
154: 	if sex.Valid {
155: 		out.Metrics.Sex = &sex.String
156: 	}
157: 	if activity.Valid {
158: 		out.Metrics.ActivityLevel = &activity.String
159: 	}
160: 	if goal.Valid {
161: 		out.Metrics.Goal = &goal.String
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	var allergiesJSON string
165: 	var dislikesJSON string
166: 	var likesJSON string
167: 	var notes sql.NullString
168: 	err = r.db.QueryRowContext(
169: 		ctx,
170: 		`SELECT diet_type, allergies_json, dislikes_json, likes_json, daily_calorie_target, notes
171:          FROM user_preferences WHERE user_id = ?`,
172: 		userID,
173: 	).Scan(&out.Preferences.DietType, &allergiesJSON, &dislikesJSON, &likesJSON, &out.Preferences.DailyCalorieTarget, &notes)
174: 	if err != nil && !errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
175: 		return dto.ProfileResponse{}, err
176: 	}
177: 	if out.Preferences.DietType == "" {
178: 		out.Preferences.DietType = "omnivore"
179: 	}
180: 	_ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(allergiesJSON), &out.Preferences.Allergies)
181: 	_ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(dislikesJSON), &out.Preferences.Dislikes)
182: 	_ = json.Unmarshal([]byte(likesJSON), &out.Preferences.Likes)
183: 	if notes.Valid {
184: 		out.Preferences.Notes = &notes.String
185: 	}
186: 
187: 	err = r.db.QueryRowContext(
188: 		ctx,
189: 		`SELECT month, currency, amount_cents FROM budgets WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY month DESC LIMIT 1`,
190: 		userID,
191: 	).Scan(&out.Budget.Month, &out.Budget.Currency, &out.Budget.AmountCents)
192: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
193: 		out.Budget.Month = time.Now().UTC().Format("2006-01")
194: 		out.Budget.Currency = "USD"
195: 		out.Budget.AmountCents = 0
196: 		return out, nil
197: 	}
198: 	if err != nil {
199: 		return dto.ProfileResponse{}, err
200: 	}
201: 	return out, nil
202: }
203: 
204: func (r *UserRepository) UpsertMetrics(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PatchMetricsRequest) error {
205: 	rowID, err := id.New("ubm")
206: 	if err != nil {
207: 		return err
208: 	}
209: 	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
210: 		ctx,
211: 		`INSERT INTO user_body_metrics (id, user_id, height_cm, weight_kg, age, sex, activity_level, goal)
212:          VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
213:          ON CONFLICT(user_id) DO UPDATE SET
214:              height_cm = excluded.height_cm,
215:              weight_kg = excluded.weight_kg,
216:              age = excluded.age,
217:              sex = excluded.sex,
218:              activity_level = excluded.activity_level,
219:              goal = excluded.goal,
220:              updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`,
221: 		rowID,
222: 		userID,
223: 		req.HeightCM,
224: 		req.WeightKG,
225: 		req.Age,
226: 		req.Sex,
227: 		req.ActivityLevel,
228: 		req.Goal,
229: 	)
230: 	return err
231: }
232: 
233: func (r *UserRepository) UpsertPreferences(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PatchPreferencesRequest) error {
234: 	rowID, err := id.New("upr")
235: 	if err != nil {

20: 	ttl    time.Duration
21: }
22: 
23: func NewTokenManager(secret string, ttl time.Duration) *TokenManager {
24: 	return &TokenManager{secret: []byte(secret), ttl: ttl}
25: }
26: 
27: func (t *TokenManager) MakeToken(userID string) (string, time.Time, error) {
28: 	expiresAt := time.Now().UTC().Add(t.ttl)
29: 	claims := Claims{UserID: userID, Exp: expiresAt.Unix()}
30: 
31: 	payload, err := json.Marshal(claims)
32: 	if err != nil {
33: 		return "", time.Time{}, err
34: 	}
35: 	payload64 := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(payload)
36: 	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, t.secret)
37: 	if _, err := mac.Write([]byte(payload64)); err != nil {
38: 		return "", time.Time{}, err
39: 	}
40: 	sig := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
41: 	return payload64 + "." + sig, expiresAt, nil
42: }
43: 
44: func (t *TokenManager) ParseToken(token string) (Claims, error) {
45: 	parts := strings.Split(token, ".")
46: 	if len(parts) != 2 {
47: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("invalid token format")
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, t.secret)
51: 	if _, err := mac.Write([]byte(parts[0])); err != nil {
52: 		return Claims{}, err
53: 	}
54: 	expected := mac.Sum(nil)
55: 	actual, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
56: 	if err != nil {
57: 		return Claims{}, err
58: 	}
59: 	if !hmac.Equal(expected, actual) {
60: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("token signature mismatch")
61: 	}
62: 
63: 	payload, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[0])
64: 	if err != nil {
65: 		return Claims{}, err
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	var claims Claims
69: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(payload, &claims); err != nil {
70: 		return Claims{}, err
71: 	}
72: 	if claims.UserID == "" || claims.Exp == 0 {
73: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("invalid token claims")
74: 	}
75: 	if time.Now().UTC().Unix() > claims.Exp {
76: 		return Claims{}, errors.New("token expired")
77: 	}
78: 	return claims, nil
79: }

1: # Backend API v1 - Auth and Profile
2: 
3: Base URL (local): `http://localhost:8080`
4: 
5: ## Error format

18: ## Authentication

20: - Token type: Bearer token from `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
21: - Token expiry: controlled by `TOKEN_TTL_HOURS` (default `24`)
22: 
23: ## Endpoints
24: 
25: ### `POST /auth/register`
26: 
27: Request:
28:

1: package middleware
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"encoding/json"
5: 	"net/http"
6: 	"strings"
7: 
8: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/auth"
9: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
10: )
11: 
12: func AuthRequired(tokens *auth.TokenManager, users *repositories.UserRepository) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
13: 	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
14: 		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
15: 			authHeader := strings.TrimSpace(r.Header.Get("Authorization"))
16: 			token := strings.TrimPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ")
17: 			if token == authHeader || token == "" {
18: 				writeAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing bearer token")
19: 				return
20: 			}
21: 
22: 			claims, err := tokens.ParseToken(token)
23: 			if err != nil {
24: 				writeAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "invalid token")
25: 				return
26: 			}
27: 
28: 			exists, err := users.ExistsByID(r.Context(), claims.UserID)
29: 			if err != nil || !exists {
30: 				writeAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "invalid token user")
31: 				return
32: 			}
33: 
34: 			ctx := WithUserID(r.Context(), claims.UserID)
35: 			next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
36: 		})
37: 	}
38: }
39: 
40: func writeAPIError(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, code, message string) {
41: 	w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
42: 	w.WriteHeader(status)
43: 	_ = json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(map[string]any{
44: 		"error": map[string]string{
45: 			"code":    code,
46: 			"message": message,
47: 		},
48: 	})
49: }

(End of file - total 49 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: 
3: import argparse
4: import json
5: import sqlite3
6: from pathlib import Path
7: 
8: 
9: def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
10:     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Import USDA FoundationFoods JSON into SQLite")
11:     parser.add_argument("--db-path", required=True, help="Path to sqlite database")
12:     parser.add_argument("--dataset-path", required=True, help="Path to USDA FoundationFoods json")
13:     return parser.parse_args()
14: 
15: 
16: def chunks(values, chunk_size: int):
17:     bucket = []
18:     for value in values:
19:         bucket.append(value)
20:         if len(bucket) >= chunk_size:
21:             yield bucket
22:             bucket = []
23:     if bucket:
24:         yield bucket
25: 
26: 
27: def main() -> None:
28:     args = parse_args()
29:     db_path = Path(args.db_path)
30:     dataset_path = Path(args.dataset_path)
31: 
32:     if not dataset_path.exists():
33:         raise SystemExit(f"Dataset not found: {dataset_path}")
34: 
35:     with dataset_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
36:         payload = json.load(f)
37: 
38:     foods = payload.get("FoundationFoods", [])
39:     dataset_name = "usda_foundation_foods"
40:     dataset_version = dataset_path.stem
41: 
42:     conn = sqlite3.connect(str(db_path))
43:     try:
44:         conn.execute("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;")
45:         conn.execute("BEGIN")
46: 
47:         food_rows = []
48:         nutrient_rows = []
49:         food_nutrient_rows = []
50: 
51:         for food in foods:
52:             if not isinstance(food, dict):
53:                 continue
54: 
55:             fdc_id = food.get("fdcId")
56:             if fdc_id is None:
57:                 continue
58: 
59:             food_rows.append(
60:                 (
61:                     int(fdc_id),
62:                     str(food.get("description") or ""),
63:                     str(food.get("foodClass") or ""),
64:                     dataset_name,
65:                     dataset_version,
66:                 )
67:             )
68: 
69:             for fn in food.get("foodNutrients", []):
70:                 if not isinstance(fn, dict):
71:                     continue
72: 
73:                 nutrient = fn.get("nutrient") or {}
74:                 nutrient_id = nutrient.get("id")
75:                 if nutrient_id is None:
76:                     continue
77: 
78:                 nutrient_rows.append(
79:                     (
80:                         int(nutrient_id),
81:                         str(nutrient.get("number") or ""),
82:                         str(nutrient.get("name") or ""),
83:                         str(nutrient.get("unitName") or ""),
84:                         nutrient.get("rank"),
85:                     )
86:                 )
87: 
88:                 derivation = fn.get("foodNutrientDerivation") or {}
89:                 source = derivation.get("foodNutrientSource") or {}
90:                 food_nutrient_rows.append(
91:                     (
92:                         fn.get("id"),
93:                         int(fdc_id),
94:                         int(nutrient_id),
95:                         fn.get("amount"),
96:                         fn.get("median"),
97:                         fn.get("min"),
98:                         fn.get("max"),
99:                         fn.get("dataPoints"),
100:                         derivation.get("code"),
101:                         derivation.get("description"),
102:                         source.get("code"),
103:                         source.get("description"),
104:                     )
105:                 )
106: 
107:         for batch in chunks(food_rows, 1000):
108:             conn.executemany(
109:                 """
110:                 INSERT INTO usda_foods (fdc_id, description, food_class, source_dataset, source_version)
111:                 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
112:                 ON CONFLICT(fdc_id) DO UPDATE SET
113:                     description = excluded.description,
114:                     food_class = excluded.food_class,
115:                     source_dataset = excluded.source_dataset,
116:                     source_version = excluded.source_version,
117:                     updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
118:                 """,
119:                 batch,
120:             )
121: 
122:         for batch in chunks(nutrient_rows, 1000):
123:             conn.executemany(
124:                 """
125:                 INSERT INTO usda_nutrients (nutrient_id, number, name, unit_name, rank)
126:                 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
127:                 ON CONFLICT(nutrient_id) DO UPDATE SET
128:                     number = excluded.number,
129:                     name = excluded.name,
130:                     unit_name = excluded.unit_name,
131:                     rank = excluded.rank
132:                 """,
133:                 batch,
134:             )
135: 
136:         for batch in chunks(food_nutrient_rows, 1000):
137:             conn.executemany(
138:                 """
139:                 INSERT INTO usda_food_nutrients (
140:                     food_nutrient_id,
141:                     fdc_id,
142:                     nutrient_id,
143:                     amount,
144:                     median,
145:                     min,
146:                     max,
147:                     data_points,
148:                     derivation_code,
149:                     derivation_description,
150:                     source_code,
151:                     source_description
152:                 )
153:                 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
154:                 ON CONFLICT(fdc_id, nutrient_id) DO UPDATE SET
155:                     food_nutrient_id = excluded.food_nutrient_id,
156:                     amount = excluded.amount,
157:                     median = excluded.median,
158:                     min = excluded.min,
159:                     max = excluded.max,
160:                     data_points = excluded.data_points,
161:                     derivation_code = excluded.derivation_code,
162:                     derivation_description = excluded.derivation_description,
163:                     source_code = excluded.source_code,
164:                     source_description = excluded.source_description,
165:                     updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
166:                 """,
167:                 batch,
168:             )
169: 
170:         conn.execute(
171:             """
172:             INSERT INTO dataset_imports (
173:                 dataset_name,
174:                 dataset_version,
175:                 source_path,
176:                 row_count_foods,
177:                 row_count_food_nutrients,
178:                 imported_at
179:             )
180:             VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP)
181:             ON CONFLICT(dataset_name) DO UPDATE SET
182:                 dataset_version = excluded.dataset_version,
183:                 source_path = excluded.source_path,
184:                 row_count_foods = excluded.row_count_foods,
185:                 row_count_food_nutrients = excluded.row_count_food_nutrients,
186:                 imported_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
187:             """,
188:             (
189:                 dataset_name,
190:                 dataset_version,
191:                 str(dataset_path),
192:                 len(food_rows),
193:                 len(food_nutrient_rows),
194:             ),
195:         )
196: 
197:         conn.commit()
198:         print(
199:             f"Imported USDA foods={len(food_rows)} nutrients={len(nutrient_rows)} "
200:             f"food_nutrients={len(food_nutrient_rows)}"
201:         )
202:     except Exception:
203:         conn.rollback()
204:         raise
205:     finally:
206:         conn.close()
207: 
208: 
209: if __name__ == "__main__":
210:     main()

(End of file - total 210 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Yes — based on QA checks, **Task 2 and your USDA schema/import changes are working end-to-end**.

- Task 2 endpoints are implemented and wired in `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:24` (`POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`, `GET /me`, `GET/PATCH /profile...`), with handlers/services in `backend/internal/transport/http/handlers/auth_handler.go:21`, `backend/internal/transport/http/handlers/profile_handler.go:21`, `backend/internal/services/auth_service.go:32`, and `backend/internal/services/profile_service.go:21`.
- Password hashing + token expiry are in place: bcrypt in `backend/internal/services/auth_service.go:50`, token exp claim validation in `backend/internal/platform/auth/token.go:75`, TTL sourced from config in `backend/internal/config/config.go:19`.
- API v1 examples are present in `docs/api/backend-v1-auth-profile.md:1`.
- USDA schema migration is valid and applied: `backend/migrations/010_usda_schema.sql:3` adds `usda_foods`, `usda_nutrients`, `usda_food_nutrients`, and `dataset_imports`.
- Local DB bootstrap/reset works with USDA import path: `scripts/db/local_db.sh:32` applies app + USDA migrations and imports via `scripts/db/import_usda.py:27`; `scripts/db/reset-local-db:6` points to full reset.
- Verified runtime results:
  - `./scripts/db/reset-local-db` succeeded and imported USDA data.
  - DB contains expected USDA tables and counts: `usda_foods=363`, `usda_nutrients=228`, `usda_food_nutrients=15193`, and matching `dataset_imports` metadata.
  - `go test ./...` passes (no failing packages).
  - Live API smoke flow passed for register/login/me/profile + all 3 profile patch endpoints on a running server.

One notable QA concern:
- `backend/cmd/dbseed/main.go:14` defaults to `./migrations/001_init_schema.sql`, but the script currently passes an absolute path so it works. Keep that in mind if someone runs `dbseed` directly without flags.

If you want, I can do a stricter negative-case sweep next (invalid token, bad payloads, weak password, duplicate email, bad budget values) and give you a compact pass/fail matrix.

1: # Session B - Backend Tasks
2: 
3: ## Owner
4: 
5: - Backend Engineer (Go API + SQLite)
6: 
7: ## Objective
8: 
9: - Deliver stable API and data layer for auth, profile, pantry, meal plans, macros, and consumption flows.
10: 
11: ## Current Status Snapshot
12: 
13: - Implemented: API bootstrap, config loading, token auth, auth handlers/services, profile handlers/services, user repository, health endpoint.
14: - Implemented: core SQLite schema, USDA schema, demo seed data, DB bootstrap helper, local reset scripts, API/setup docs.
15: - Not implemented: pantry endpoints, recipe endpoints, plan endpoints, purchases endpoints, consumption endpoint, macro aggregation endpoints, backend tests.
16: 
17: ## Ordered Task List
18: 
19: ### 1) Schema and setup first (0:30-1:30)
20: 
21: - [x] `P0` Implement SQLite schema + migrations for:
22:   - users
23:   - user_body_metrics
24:   - user_preferences
25:   - budgets
26:   - purchases
27:   - USDA foods / nutrients
28:   - recipes
29:   - recipe_ingredients
30:   - pantry_items
31:   - meal_plans
32:   - plan_meals
33:   - consumption_log
34:   - chat_messages
35:   - favorites (optional table behind feature flag/time)
36: - [x] `P0` Add one-command local DB setup/reset path.
37: - [x] `P0` Seed ingredients and minimal recipes for deterministic demo.
38: 
39: ### 2) Auth and profile APIs (1:30-2:30)
40: 
41: - [x] `P0` Auth endpoints: `POST /auth/register`, `POST /auth/login`, `GET /me`.
42: - [x] `P0` Password hashing and token/session expiry rules.
43: - [x] `P0` Profile endpoints for metrics, preferences, budget:
44:   - `GET /profile`
45:   - `PATCH /profile/metrics`
46:   - `PATCH /profile/preferences`
47:   - `PATCH /profile/budget`
48: - [x] `P0` Publish API v1 examples for frontend and AI sessions.
49: 
50: ### 3) Pantry and recipes APIs (2:30-3:30)
51: 
52: - [ ] `P0` `GET /ingredients/search?q=` for pantry add flow.
53: - [ ] `P0` `POST /pantry/items` (ingredient + quantity + unit).
54: - [ ] `P0` `PATCH /pantry/items/:id` (increment/decrement quantity).
55: - [ ] `P0` `DELETE /pantry/items/:id`.
56: - [ ] `P0` `GET /recipes/:id` with ingredient quantities and macros.
57: - [ ] `P1` Purchases endpoints for budget tracking:
58:   - `POST /purchases`
59:   - `GET /budget/summary`
60: 
61: ### 4) Plan persistence and macro aggregation (3:30-5:00)
62: 
63: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/proposal` ingestion endpoint for AI-normalized payload.
64: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/accept` persists to meal plans.
65: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plans/:id/decline` archives/rejects and supports regeneration flow.
66: - [ ] `P0` `GET /plans/week?start=` returns 7-day calendar payload.
67: - [ ] `P0` Macro totals service in responses:
68:   - per meal
69:   - per day
70:   - per week
71: 
72: ### 5) Consumption and pantry deduction (5:00-6:00)
73: 
74: - [ ] `P0` `POST /plan-meals/:id/consume` marks consumed with timestamp.
75: - [ ] `P0` Deduct pantry ingredients by recipe quantities.
76: - [ ] `P0` Prevent negative pantry values (clamp at zero + warning field).
77: - [ ] `P0` Log deductions to `consumption_log` for audit/debug.
78: 
79: ### 6) Hardening for demo (6:00-7:00)
80: 
81: - [x] `P0` Add consistent error format for frontend handling.
82: - [ ] `P0` Add lightweight request logging and key action tracing.
83: - [ ] `P0` Add smoke checks for auth -> plan accept -> consume flow.
84: - [ ] `P1` Add favorite proposal endpoint if time remains.
85: 
86: ## Contracts Needed From Others
87: 
88: - PM: frozen priority and final scope boundaries.
89: - AI: normalized proposal JSON schema and ingredient matching policy.
90: - Frontend: required response fields per screen.
91: - QA: top-priority test scenarios and failure expectations.
92: 
93: ## Risks
94: 
95: - AI names not matching ingredient table.
96: - Unit mismatches (g/ml/piece) creating deduction inaccuracies.
97: - Time overrun from building too many endpoints before core path is stable.
98: 
99: ## Done Criteria
100: 
101: - API supports full happy path from auth to pantry auto-deduction.
102: - Weekly plan endpoint returns 7-day, 4-section layout with macro totals.
103: - DB setup/seed is reproducible in one command.
104: - Frontend can run without backend contract guesswork.
105: 
106: ## Immediate Backend Focus
107: 
108: - Pantry search/add/update/delete endpoints.
109: - Recipe read payloads with ingredient names sourced from USDA foods.
110: - Weekly plan persistence/read model and accept/decline lifecycle.
111: - Consumption endpoint plus pantry deduction and logging.

(End of file - total 111 lines)
</content>

1: package router
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"net/http"
5: 
6: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/platform/auth"

12: type Handlers struct {
13: 	Health  *handlers.HealthHandler
14: 	Auth    *handlers.AuthHandler
15: 	Profile *handlers.ProfileHandler
16: 	Pantry  *handlers.PantryHandler
17: 	Recipe  *handlers.RecipeHandler
18: }
19: 
20: func New(h Handlers, tokens *auth.TokenManager, users *repositories.UserRepository) http.Handler {
21: 	mux := http.NewServeMux()
22: 
23: 	authRequired := middleware.AuthRequired(tokens, users)
24: 
25: 	mux.HandleFunc("GET /health", h.Health.GetHealth)
26: 	mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/register", h.Auth.Register)
27: 	mux.HandleFunc("POST /auth/login", h.Auth.Login)
28: 	mux.Handle("GET /ingredients/search", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Pantry.SearchFoods)))
29: 	mux.Handle("GET /me", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Auth.Me)))
30: 	mux.Handle("GET /profile", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Profile.GetProfile)))
31: 	mux.Handle("PATCH /profile/metrics", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Profile.PatchMetrics)))
32: 	mux.Handle("PATCH /profile/preferences", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Profile.PatchPreferences)))
33: 	mux.Handle("PATCH /profile/budget", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Profile.PatchBudget)))
34: 	mux.Handle("GET /pantry/items", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Pantry.ListPantryItems)))
35: 	mux.Handle("POST /pantry/items", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Pantry.CreatePantryItem)))
36: 	mux.Handle("PATCH /pantry/items/{id}", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Pantry.PatchPantryItem)))
37: 	mux.Handle("DELETE /pantry/items/{id}", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Pantry.DeletePantryItem)))
38: 	mux.Handle("GET /recipes/{id}", authRequired(http.HandlerFunc(h.Recipe.GetRecipe)))
39: 
40: 	return middleware.CORS(middleware.Logging(mux))
41: }

1: package handlers

10: )
11: 
12: type PantryHandler struct {
13: 	pantry *services.PantryService
14: }
15: 
16: func NewPantryHandler(pantry *services.PantryService) *PantryHandler {
17: 	return &PantryHandler{pantry: pantry}
18: }
19: 
20: func (h *PantryHandler) SearchFoods(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
21: 	items, err := h.pantry.SearchFoods(r.Context(), r.URL.Query().Get("q"))
22: 	if err != nil {
23: 		if errors.Is(err, services.ErrInvalidSearchQuery) {
24: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusBadRequest, "validation_error", err.Error())
25: 			return
26: 		}
27: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to search foods")
28: 		return
29: 	}
30: 
31: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, dto.FoodSearchResponse{Items: items})
32: }
33: 
34: func (h *PantryHandler) ListPantryItems(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
35: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
36: 	if !ok {
37: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")
38: 		return
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	items, err := h.pantry.ListPantryItems(r.Context(), userID)
42: 	if err != nil {
43: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to load pantry items")
44: 		return
45: 	}
46: 
47: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, dto.PantryItemsResponse{Items: items})
48: }
49: 
50: func (h *PantryHandler) CreatePantryItem(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
51: 	userID, ok := middleware.UserIDFromContext(r.Context())
52: 	if !ok {
53: 		WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusUnauthorized, "unauthorized", "missing auth context")
54: 		return
55: 	}
56:

2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"

13: type PantryRepository struct {
14: 	db *sql.DB
15: }
16: 
17: func NewPantryRepository(db *sql.DB) *PantryRepository {
18: 	return &PantryRepository{db: db}
19: }
20: 
21: func (r *PantryRepository) ListByUserID(ctx context.Context, userID string) ([]dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
22: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
23: 		ctx,
24: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, p.unit, f.fdc_id, f.description, COALESCE(f.food_class, '')
25: 		 FROM pantry_items p
26: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
27: 		 WHERE p.user_id = ?
28: 		 ORDER BY f.description ASC`,
29: 		userID,
30: 	)
31: 	if err != nil {
32: 		return nil, err
33: 	}
34: 	defer rows.Close()
35: 
36: 	items := make([]dto.PantryItemResponse, 0)
37: 	for rows.Next() {
38: 		var item dto.PantryItemResponse
39: 		if err := rows.Scan(&item.ID, &item.Quantity, &item.Unit, &item.Food.FDCID, &item.Food.Description, &item.Food.FoodClass); err != nil {
40: 			return nil, err
41: 		}
42: 		items = append(items, item)
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	return items, rows.Err()
46: }
47: 
48: func (r *PantryRepository) Upsert(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PantryItemRequest) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
49: 	itemID, err := id.New("pnt")
50: 	if err != nil {
51: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
52: 	}
53: 
54: 	unit := strings.TrimSpace(req.Unit)
55: 	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
56: 		ctx,
57: 		`INSERT INTO pantry_items (id, user_id, fdc_id, quantity, unit)
58: 		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
59: 		 ON CONFLICT(user_id, fdc_id, unit) DO UPDATE SET
60: 			 quantity = pantry_items.quantity + excluded.quantity,
61: 			 updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`,
62: 		itemID,
63: 		userID,
64: 		req.FDCID,
65: 		req.Quantity,
66: 		unit,
67: 	)
68: 	if err != nil {
69: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	return r.GetByUserAndFood(ctx, userID, req.FDCID, unit)
73: }
74: 
75: func (r *PantryRepository) GetByUserAndFood(ctx context.Context, userID string, fdcID int64, unit string) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
76: 	var item dto.PantryItemResponse
77: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
78: 		ctx,
79: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, p.unit, f.fdc_id, f.description, COALESCE(f.food_class, '')
80: 		 FROM pantry_items p
81: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
82: 		 WHERE p.user_id = ? AND p.fdc_id = ? AND p.unit = ?`,
83: 		userID,
84: 		fdcID,
85: 		unit,
86: 	).Scan(&item.ID, &item.Quantity, &item.Unit, &item.Food.FDCID, &item.Food.Description, &item.Food.FoodClass)
87: 	return item, err
88: }
89: 
90: func (r *PantryRepository) PatchQuantity(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string, delta float64) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
91: 	_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
92: 		ctx,
93: 		`UPDATE pantry_items
94: 		 SET quantity = CASE
95: 			 WHEN quantity + ? < 0 THEN 0
96: 			 ELSE quantity + ?
97: 		 END,
98: 		 updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
99: 		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
100: 		delta,
101: 		delta,
102: 		itemID,
103: 		userID,
104: 	)
105: 	if err != nil {
106: 		return dto.PantryItemResponse{}, err
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	return r.GetByID(ctx, userID, itemID)
110: }
111: 
112: func (r *PantryRepository) GetByID(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string) (dto.PantryItemResponse, error) {
113: 	var item dto.PantryItemResponse
114: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
115: 		ctx,
116: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, p.unit, f.fdc_id, f.description, COALESCE(f.food_class, '')
117: 		 FROM pantry_items p
118: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
119: 		 WHERE p.id = ? AND p.user_id = ?`,
120: 		itemID,
121: 		userID,
122: 	).Scan(&item.ID, &item.Quantity, &item.Unit, &item.Food.FDCID, &item.Food.Description, &item.Food.FoodClass)
123: 	return item, err
124: }
125: 
126: func (r *PantryRepository) Delete(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string) error {
127: 	result, err := r.db.ExecContext(ctx, `DELETE FROM pantry_items WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`, itemID, userID)
128: 	if err != nil {
129: 		return err
130: 	}
131: 	affected, err := result.RowsAffected()
132: 	if err != nil {
133: 		return err
134: 	}
135: 	if affected == 0 {
136: 		return sql.ErrNoRows
137: 	}
138: 	return nil
139: }
140: 
141: func (r *PantryRepository) Exists(ctx context.Context, userID, itemID string) (bool, error) {
142: 	_, err := r.GetByID(ctx, userID, itemID)
143: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
144: 		return false, nil
145: 	}
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return false, err
148: 	}
149: 	return true, nil
150: }

49:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
50:     UNIQUE (user_id, month)
51: );
52: 
53: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS purchases (
54:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
55:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
56:     budget_id TEXT,
57:     fdc_id INTEGER,
58:     description TEXT,
59:     quantity REAL,
60:     unit TEXT,
61:     cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (cost_cents >= 0),
62:     purchased_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
63:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
64:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
65:     FOREIGN KEY (budget_id) REFERENCES budgets(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
66:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE SET NULL
67: );
68: 
69: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipes (
70:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
71:     name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
72:     meal_type TEXT NOT NULL,
73:     servings INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (servings > 0),
74:     instructions TEXT,
75:     total_kcal REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
76:     total_protein_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
77:     total_carbs_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
78:     total_fat_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
79:     estimated_cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
80:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
81:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
82:     CHECK (meal_type IN ('breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner', 'snacks'))
83: );
84: 
85: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS recipe_ingredients (
86:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
87:     recipe_id TEXT NOT NULL,
88:     fdc_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
89:     quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity >= 0),
90:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
91:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
92:     FOREIGN KEY (recipe_id) REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
93:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
94:     UNIQUE (recipe_id, fdc_id, unit)
95: );
96: 
97: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pantry_items (
98:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
99:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
100:     fdc_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
101:     quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity >= 0),
102:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
103:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
104:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
105:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
106:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE RESTRICT,
107:     UNIQUE (user_id, fdc_id, unit)
108: );
109: 
110: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meal_plans (
111:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
112:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
113:     period_type TEXT NOT NULL,
114:     start_date DATE NOT NULL,
115:     end_date DATE NOT NULL,
116:     status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'accepted',
117:     source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ai',
118:     proposal_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
119:     ai_cost_cents_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (ai_cost_cents_total >= 0),
120:     notes TEXT,
121:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
122:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
123:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
124:     CHECK (period_type IN ('day', 'week', 'month')),
125:     CHECK (status IN ('proposal', 'accepted', 'declined', 'archived')),
126:     CHECK (source IN ('ai', 'fallback', 'manual')),
127:     CHECK (start_date <= end_date)
128: );
129: 
130: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS plan_meals (
131:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
132:     meal_plan_id TEXT NOT NULL,
133:     recipe_id TEXT,
134:     scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL,
135:     meal_section TEXT NOT NULL,
136:     recipe_name TEXT NOT NULL,
137:     servings REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 1 CHECK (servings > 0),
138:     kcal REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
139:     protein_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
140:     carbs_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
141:     fat_g REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
142:     estimated_cost_cents INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
143:     is_consumed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (is_consumed IN (0, 1)),
144:     consumed_at DATETIME,
145:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
146:     updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
147:     FOREIGN KEY (meal_plan_id) REFERENCES meal_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
148:     FOREIGN KEY (recipe_id) REFERENCES recipes(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
149:     CHECK (meal_section IN ('breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner', 'snacks')),
150:     UNIQUE (meal_plan_id, scheduled_date, meal_section)
151: );
152: 
153: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS consumption_log (
154:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
155:     plan_meal_id TEXT,
156:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
157:     fdc_id INTEGER,
158:     pantry_item_id TEXT,
159:     quantity_deducted REAL NOT NULL CHECK (quantity_deducted >= 0),
160:     unit TEXT NOT NULL,
161:     before_quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (before_quantity >= 0),
162:     after_quantity REAL NOT NULL CHECK (after_quantity >= 0),
163:     warning TEXT,
164:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
165:     FOREIGN KEY (plan_meal_id) REFERENCES plan_meals(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
166:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
167:     FOREIGN KEY (fdc_id) REFERENCES usda_foods(fdc_id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
168:     FOREIGN KEY (pantry_item_id) REFERENCES pantry_items(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
169: );
170: 
171: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS chat_messages (
172:     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
173:     user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
174:     meal_plan_id TEXT,
175:     role TEXT NOT NULL,
176:     action TEXT,
177:     content TEXT NOT NULL,
178:     metadata_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
179:     created_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
180:     FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
181:     FOREIGN KEY (meal_plan_id) REFERENCES meal_plans(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
182:     CHECK (role IN ('system', 'user', 'assistant', 'tool'))
183: );
184: 
185: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_users_email ON users(email);
186: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pantry_items_user_id ON pantry_items(user_id);
187: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pantry_items_fdc_id ON pantry_items(fdc_id);
188: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_meal_plans_user_period ON meal_plans(user_id, period_type, start_date);
189: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_plan_meals_meal_plan_id ON plan_meals(meal_plan_id);
190: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_plan_meals_schedule ON plan_meals(scheduled_date, meal_section);
191: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_consumption_log_user_created_at ON consumption_log(user_id, created_at);
192: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_chat_messages_user_created_at ON chat_messages(user_id, created_at);

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package repositories
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"strings"
7: 
8: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
9: )
10: 
11: type FoodRepository struct {
12: 	db *sql.DB
13: }
14: 
15: func NewFoodRepository(db *sql.DB) *FoodRepository {
16: 	return &FoodRepository{db: db}
17: }
18: 
19: func (r *FoodRepository) SearchFoods(ctx context.Context, query string, limit int) ([]dto.FoodSearchItem, error) {
20: 	query = strings.TrimSpace(query)
21: 	if limit <= 0 {
22: 		limit = 20
23: 	}
24: 
25: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
26: 		ctx,
27: 		`SELECT fdc_id, description, COALESCE(food_class, '')
28: 		 FROM usda_foods
29: 		 WHERE description LIKE ?
30: 		 ORDER BY description ASC
31: 		 LIMIT ?`,
32: 		"%"+query+"%",
33: 		limit,
34: 	)
35: 	if err != nil {
36: 		return nil, err
37: 	}
38: 	defer rows.Close()
39: 
40: 	items := make([]dto.FoodSearchItem, 0)
41: 	for rows.Next() {
42: 		var item dto.FoodSearchItem
43: 		if err := rows.Scan(&item.FDCID, &item.Description, &item.FoodClass); err != nil {
44: 			return nil, err
45: 		}
46: 		items = append(items, item)
47: 	}
48: 
49: 	return items, rows.Err()
50: }
51: 
52: func (r *FoodRepository) ExistsByFDCID(ctx context.Context, fdcID int64) (bool, error) {
53: 	var n int
54: 	if err := r.db.QueryRowContext(ctx, `SELECT COUNT(1) FROM usda_foods WHERE fdc_id = ?`, fdcID).Scan(&n); err != nil {
55: 		return false, err
56: 	}
57: 	return n > 0, nil
58: }

(End of file - total 58 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Mostly yes: **step 3 P0 is implemented and working**, but **the P1 purchases part is not done**.

What I verified:
- `GET /ingredients/search?q=` exists and works: `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:28`, backed by `backend/internal/repositories/food_repository.go:19`.
- `POST /pantry/items` exists and works: `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:35`, handler/service/repo in `pantry_handler.go:50`, `pantry_service.go:41`, `pantry_repository.go:48`.
- `PATCH /pantry/items/:id` exists and works: `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:36`, with quantity update in `pantry_repository.go:90`.
- `DELETE /pantry/items/:id` exists and works: `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:37`, delete logic in `pantry_repository.go:126`.
- `GET /recipes/:id` exists and works, including ingredient quantities and macros: `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:38`, `recipe_repository.go:18`.

Live smoke test result:
- Registered a fresh user.
- Searched foods successfully.
- Added pantry item successfully.
- Patched pantry quantity successfully.
- Deleted pantry item successfully.
- Fetched `rcp_breakfast_oats` successfully with 3 ingredients and macros.

Important caveats:
- **Purchases endpoints are not implemented**. I found no code for `POST /purchases` or `GET /budget/summary`, so step 3 is **not fully complete** if you include the P1 items.
- The checklist in `tasks/backend.md` still shows all step 3 items unchecked, so the task file is out of sync with the code.
- Separate issue: the seeded demo user cannot be used for login because `backend/seeds/001_seed_demo.sql:5` uses a placeholder bcrypt hash. That does not block step 3 itself, but it affects demo usability.

Verdict:
- **Step 3 P0: done**
- **Step 3 overall including P1: not fully done**

parts.text:
All of **step 4 P0 is still missing**.

Missing endpoints:
- `POST /plans/proposal`
- `POST /plans/:id/accept`
- `POST /plans/:id/decline`
- `GET /plans/week?start=`

What I checked:
- No plan routes exist in `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:25-39`.
- No plan handlers/services/repositories exist under `backend/internal`.
- No DTOs exist for proposal ingestion, accept/decline, or weekly plan responses in `backend/internal/transport/http/dto/types.go:1-148`.

Macro aggregation is also missing:
- No meal/day/week macro response models.
- No plan read model that returns a 7-day calendar payload.
- No aggregation service code beyond recipe-level macros in `backend/internal/repositories/recipe_repository.go:18`.

One more sign this is still open:
- `tasks/backend.md:15` explicitly still says plan endpoints and macro aggregation endpoints are not implemented.

So the missing pieces from step 4 P0 are:
1. Proposal ingestion API and payload model.
2. Persistence logic for proposals into `meal_plans` and `plan_meals`.
3. Accept/decline lifecycle handling and status transitions.
4. Weekly plan query endpoint with 7-day grouped response.
5. Macro total calculations per meal, per day, and per week.

1: package services
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"errors"
7: 	"sort"
8: 	"strings"
9: 	"time"
10: 
11: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
12: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
13: )
14: 
15: var (
16: 	ErrInvalidPlanType    = errors.New("periodType must be one of day, week, month")
17: 	ErrInvalidStartDate   = errors.New("startDate must use YYYY-MM-DD")
18: 	ErrInvalidEndDate     = errors.New("endDate must use YYYY-MM-DD")
19: 	ErrInvalidPlanMeals   = errors.New("at least one meal is required")
20: 	ErrInvalidMealSection = errors.New("mealSection must be one of breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks")
21: 	ErrInvalidPlanStatus  = errors.New("plan not found")
22: 	ErrPlanNotProposal    = errors.New("plan is not in proposal status")
23: 	ErrWeekStartDate      = errors.New("start must use YYYY-MM-DD")
24: )
25: 
26: type PlanService struct {
27: 	plans *repositories.PlanRepository
28: }
29: 
30: func NewPlanService(plans *repositories.PlanRepository) *PlanService {
31: 	return &PlanService{plans: plans}
32: }
33: 
34: func (s *PlanService) CreateProposal(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PlanProposalRequest) (dto.ProposalResponse, error) {
35: 	if err := validatePlanProposal(req); err != nil {
36: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
37: 	}
38: 	if req.Source == "" {
39: 		req.Source = "ai"
40: 	}
41: 	if req.ProposalVersion == 0 {
42: 		req.ProposalVersion = 1
43: 	}
44: 	if req.EndDate == "" {
45: 		req.EndDate = req.StartDate
46: 	}
47: 
48: 	plan, err := s.plans.CreateProposal(ctx, userID, req)
49: 	if err != nil {
50: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	return buildProposalResponse([]repositories.StoredPlan{plan}), nil
54: }
55: 
56: func (s *PlanService) AcceptProposal(ctx context.Context, userID, planID string) (dto.ProposalResponse, error) {
57: 	plan, err := s.plans.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
58: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
59: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrInvalidPlanStatus
60: 	}
61: 	if err != nil {
62: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
63: 	}
64: 	if plan.Status != "proposal" {
65: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrPlanNotProposal
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	updated, err := s.plans.UpdateStatus(ctx, userID, planID, "accepted", "")
69: 	if err != nil {
70: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	return buildProposalResponse([]repositories.StoredPlan{updated}), nil
74: }
75: 
76: func (s *PlanService) DeclineProposal(ctx context.Context, userID, planID, reason string) (dto.ProposalResponse, error) {
77: 	plan, err := s.plans.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
78: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
79: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrInvalidPlanStatus
80: 	}
81: 	if err != nil {
82: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
83: 	}
84: 	if plan.Status != "proposal" {
85: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, ErrPlanNotProposal
86: 	}
87: 
88: 	note := ""
89: 	if strings.TrimSpace(reason) != "" {
90: 		note = "decline_reason: " + strings.TrimSpace(reason)
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	updated, err := s.plans.UpdateStatus(ctx, userID, planID, "declined", note)
94: 	if err != nil {
95: 		return dto.ProposalResponse{}, err
96: 	}
97: 
98: 	return buildProposalResponse([]repositories.StoredPlan{updated}), nil
99: }
100: 
101: func (s *PlanService) GetWeekPlan(ctx context.Context, userID, startDate string) (dto.WeekPlanResponse, error) {
102: 	start, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", startDate)
103: 	if err != nil {
104: 		return dto.WeekPlanResponse{}, ErrWeekStartDate
105: 	}
106: 	endDate := start.AddDate(0, 0, 6).Format("2006-01-02")
107: 	plans, err := s.plans.ListAcceptedPlansForRange(ctx, userID, startDate, endDate)
108: 	if err != nil {
109: 		return dto.WeekPlanResponse{}, err
110: 	}
111: 	return buildWeekPlanResponse(plans, startDate, endDate), nil
112: }
113: 
114: func validatePlanProposal(req dto.PlanProposalRequest) error {
115: 	switch req.PeriodType {
116: 	case "day", "week", "month":
117: 	default:
118: 		return ErrInvalidPlanType
119: 	}
120: 	if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.StartDate); err != nil {
121: 		return ErrInvalidStartDate
122: 	}
123: 	if req.EndDate != "" {
124: 		if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", req.EndDate); err != nil {
125: 			return ErrInvalidEndDate
126: 		}
127: 	}
128: 	if len(req.Meals) == 0 {
129: 		return ErrInvalidPlanMeals
130: 	}
131: 	for _, meal := range req.Meals {
132: 		switch meal.MealSection {
133: 		case "breakfast", "lunch", "dinner", "snacks":
134: 		default:
135: 			return ErrInvalidMealSection
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 	return nil
139: }
140: 
141: func buildProposalResponse(plans []repositories.StoredPlan) dto.ProposalResponse {
142: 	week := buildWeekPlanResponse(plans, plans[0].StartDate, plans[0].EndDate)
143: 	return dto.ProposalResponse{
144: 		Plan:       week.Plans[0],
145: 		Days:       week.Days,
146: 		WeekTotals: week.WeekTotals,
147: 	}
148: }
149: 
150: func buildWeekPlanResponse(plans []repositories.StoredPlan, startDate, endDate string) dto.WeekPlanResponse {
151: 	planSummaries := make([]dto.PlanSummaryResponse, 0, len(plans))
152: 	allMeals := make([]repositories.StoredPlanMeal, 0)
153: 	for _, plan := range plans {
154: 		planSummaries = append(planSummaries, dto.PlanSummaryResponse{
155: 			ID:               plan.ID,
156: 			PeriodType:       plan.PeriodType,
157: 			StartDate:        plan.StartDate,
158: 			EndDate:          plan.EndDate,
159: 			Status:           plan.Status,
160: 			Source:           plan.Source,
161: 			ProposalVersion:  plan.ProposalVersion,
162: 			AICostCentsTotal: plan.AICostCentsTotal,
163: 			Notes:            plan.Notes,
164: 		})
165: 		allMeals = append(allMeals, plan.Meals...)
166: 	}
167: 
168: 	sort.Slice(allMeals, func(i, j int) bool {
169: 		if allMeals[i].ScheduledDate == allMeals[j].ScheduledDate {
170: 			return allMeals[i].MealSection < allMeals[j].MealSection
171: 		}
172: 		return allMeals[i].ScheduledDate < allMeals[j].ScheduledDate
173: 	})
174: 
175: 	days := make([]dto.PlanDayResponse, 0)
176: 	weeklyTotals := dto.RecipeMacrosResponse{}
177: 	mealIndex := map[string]repositories.StoredPlanMeal{}
178: 	for _, meal := range allMeals {
179: 		mealIndex[meal.ScheduledDate+"|"+meal.MealSection] = meal
180: 		weeklyTotals.Calories += meal.Calories
181: 		weeklyTotals.ProteinG += meal.ProteinG
182: 		weeklyTotals.CarbsG += meal.CarbsG
183: 		weeklyTotals.FatG += meal.FatG
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	start, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", startDate)
187: 	if err != nil {
188: 		return dto.WeekPlanResponse{Plans: planSummaries}
189: 	}
190: 	end, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", endDate)
191: 	if err != nil {
192: 		end = start
193: 	}
194: 
195: 	for d := start; !d.After(end); d = d.AddDate(0, 0, 1) {
196: 		date := d.Format("2006-01-02")
197: 		day := dto.PlanDayResponse{Date: date}
198: 		for _, section := range []string{"breakfast", "lunch", "dinner", "snacks"} {
199: 			meal, ok := mealIndex[date+"|"+section]
200: 			if !ok {
201: 				continue
202: 			}
203: 			res := mealToDTO(meal)
204: 			switch section {
205: 			case "breakfast":
206: 				day.Sections.Breakfast = &res
207: 			case "lunch":
208: 				day.Sections.Lunch = &res
209: 			case "dinner":
210: 				day.Sections.Dinner = &res
211: 			case "snacks":
212: 				day.Sections.Snacks = &res
213: 			}
214: 			day.Totals.Calories += meal.Calories
215: 			day.Totals.ProteinG += meal.ProteinG
216: 			day.Totals.CarbsG += meal.CarbsG
217: 			day.Totals.FatG += meal.FatG
218: 		}
219: 		days = append(days, day)
220: 	}
221: 
222: 	return dto.WeekPlanResponse{Plans: planSummaries, Days: days, WeekTotals: weeklyTotals}
223: }
224: 
225: func mealToDTO(meal repositories.StoredPlanMeal) dto.PlanMealResponse {
226: 	return dto.PlanMealResponse{
227: 		ID:                 meal.ID,
228: 		RecipeID:           meal.RecipeID,
229: 		RecipeName:         meal.RecipeName,
230: 		ScheduledDate:      meal.ScheduledDate,
231: 		MealSection:        meal.MealSection,
232: 		Servings:           meal.Servings,
233: 		EstimatedCostCents: meal.EstimatedCostCents,
234: 		Macros: dto.RecipeMacrosResponse{
235: 			Calories: meal.Calories,
236: 			ProteinG: meal.ProteinG,
237: 			CarbsG:   meal.CarbsG,
238: 			FatG:     meal.FatG,
239: 		},
240: 		IsConsumed: meal.IsConsumed,
241: 		ConsumedAt: meal.ConsumedAt,
242: 	}
243: }

(End of file - total 243 lines)
</content>
