46: 
47: func NewPlanRepository(db *sql.DB) *PlanRepository {
48: 	return &PlanRepository{db: db}
49: }
50: 
51: func (r *PlanRepository) CreateProposal(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PlanProposalRequest) (StoredPlan, error) {
52: 	planID, err := id.New("pln")
53: 	if err != nil {
54: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
55: 	}
56: 
57: 	tx, err := r.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
58: 	if err != nil {
59: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
60: 	}
61: 	defer tx.Rollback()
62: 
63: 	_, err = tx.ExecContext(
64: 		ctx,
65: 		`INSERT INTO meal_plans (id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, notes)
66: 		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'proposal', ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
67: 		planID,
68: 		userID,
69: 		req.PeriodType,
70: 		req.StartDate,
71: 		req.EndDate,
72: 		req.Source,
73: 		req.ProposalVersion,
74: 		req.AICostCentsTotal,
75: 		req.Notes,
76: 	)
77: 	if err != nil {
78: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
79: 	}
80: 
81: 	for _, meal := range req.Meals {
82: 		mealID, err := id.New("pm")
83: 		if err != nil {
84: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
85: 		}
86: 
87: 		_, err = tx.ExecContext(
88: 			ctx,
89: 			`INSERT INTO plan_meals (
90: 				id, meal_plan_id, recipe_id, scheduled_date, meal_section, recipe_name,
91: 				servings, kcal, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, estimated_cost_cents
92: 			 ) VALUES (?, ?, NULLIF(?, ''), ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
93: 			mealID,
94: 			planID,
95: 			meal.RecipeID,
96: 			meal.ScheduledDate,
97: 			meal.MealSection,
98: 			meal.RecipeName,
99: 			meal.Servings,
100: 			meal.Macros.Calories,
101: 			meal.Macros.ProteinG,
102: 			meal.Macros.CarbsG,
103: 			meal.Macros.FatG,
104: 			meal.EstimatedCostCents,
105: 		)
106: 		if err != nil {
107: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
108: 		}
109: 	}
110: 
111: 	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
112: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
113: 	}
114: 
115: 	return r.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
116: }
117: 
118: func (r *PlanRepository) GetPlanByID(ctx context.Context, userID, planID string) (StoredPlan, error) {
119: 	var plan StoredPlan
120: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
121: 		ctx,
122: 		`SELECT id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, COALESCE(notes, '')
123: 		 FROM meal_plans
124: 		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
125: 		planID,
126: 		userID,
127: 	).Scan(
128: 		&plan.ID,
129: 		&plan.UserID,
130: 		&plan.PeriodType,
131: 		&plan.StartDate,
132: 		&plan.EndDate,
133: 		&plan.Status,
134: 		&plan.Source,
135: 		&plan.ProposalVersion,
136: 		&plan.AICostCentsTotal,
137: 		&plan.Notes,
138: 	)
139: 	if err != nil {
140: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
141: 	}
142: 
143: 	meals, err := r.listMealsForPlan(ctx, planID)
144: 	if err != nil {
145: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
146: 	}
147: 	plan.Meals = meals
148: 	return plan, nil
149: }
150: 
151: func (r *PlanRepository) UpdateStatus(ctx context.Context, userID, planID, status, noteSuffix string) (StoredPlan, error) {
152: 	if strings.TrimSpace(noteSuffix) == "" {
153: 		_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
154: 			ctx,
155: 			`UPDATE meal_plans SET status = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
156: 			status,
157: 			planID,
158: 			userID,
159: 		)
160: 		if err != nil {
161: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
162: 		}
163: 	} else {
164: 		_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
165: 			ctx,
166: 			`UPDATE meal_plans
167: 			 SET status = ?,
168: 			     notes = TRIM(COALESCE(notes, '') || CASE WHEN COALESCE(notes, '') = '' THEN '' ELSE ' | ' END || ?),
169: 			     updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
170: 			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
171: 			status,
172: 			noteSuffix,
173: 			planID,
174: 			userID,
175: 		)
176: 		if err != nil {
177: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
178: 		}
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	return r.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
182: }
183: 
184: func (r *PlanRepository) ListAcceptedPlansForRange(ctx context.Context, userID, startDate, endDate string) ([]StoredPlan, error) {
185: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
186: 		ctx,
187: 		`SELECT id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, COALESCE(notes, '')
188: 		 FROM meal_plans
189: 		 WHERE user_id = ? AND status = 'accepted' AND start_date <= ? AND end_date >= ?
190: 		 ORDER BY start_date ASC, created_at ASC`,
191: 		userID,
192: 		endDate,
193: 		startDate,
194: 	)
195: 	if err != nil {
196: 		return nil, err
197: 	}
198: 	defer rows.Close()
199: 
200: 	plans := make([]StoredPlan, 0)
201: 	for rows.Next() {
202: 		var plan StoredPlan
203: 		if err := rows.Scan(
204: 			&plan.ID,
205: 			&plan.UserID,
206: 			&plan.PeriodType,
207: 			&plan.StartDate,
208: 			&plan.EndDate,
209: 			&plan.Status,
210: 			&plan.Source,
211: 			&plan.ProposalVersion,
212: 			&plan.AICostCentsTotal,
213: 			&plan.Notes,
214: 		); err != nil {
215: 			return nil, err
216: 		}
217: 		plans = append(plans, plan)
218: 	}
219: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
220: 		return nil, err
221: 	}
222: 
223: 	for i := range plans {
224: 		meals, err := r.listMealsForPlanInRange(ctx, plans[i].ID, startDate, endDate)
225: 		if err != nil {
226: 			return nil, err
227: 		}
228: 		plans[i].Meals = meals
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	return plans, nil
232: }
233: 
234: func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlan(ctx context.Context, planID string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
235: 	return r.listMeals(ctx, `WHERE meal_plan_id = ?`, planID)
236: }
237: 
238: func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlanInRange(ctx context.Context, planID, startDate, endDate string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
239: 	return r.listMeals(ctx, `WHERE meal_plan_id = ? AND scheduled_date >= ? AND scheduled_date <= ?`, planID, startDate, endDate)
240: }
241: 
242: func (r *PlanRepository) listMeals(ctx context.Context, whereClause string, args ...any) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
243: 	query := fmt.Sprintf(`SELECT id, COALESCE(recipe_id, ''), recipe_name, scheduled_date, meal_section, servings,
244: 		 COALESCE(kcal, 0), COALESCE(protein_g, 0), COALESCE(carbs_g, 0), COALESCE(fat_g, 0), estimated_cost_cents,
245: 		 is_consumed, COALESCE(consumed_at, '')
246: 		 FROM plan_meals %s ORDER BY scheduled_date ASC, meal_section ASC`, whereClause)
247: 
248: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, args...)
249: 	if err != nil {
250: 		return nil, err
251: 	}
252: 	defer rows.Close()
253: 
254: 	meals := make([]StoredPlanMeal, 0)
255: 	for rows.Next() {
256: 		var meal StoredPlanMeal
257: 		var consumedInt int
258: 		if err := rows.Scan(
259: 			&meal.ID,
260: 			&meal.RecipeID,
261: 			&meal.RecipeName,
262: 			&meal.ScheduledDate,
263: 			&meal.MealSection,
264: 			&meal.Servings,
265: 			&meal.Calories,
266: 			&meal.ProteinG,
267: 			&meal.CarbsG,
268: 			&meal.FatG,
269: 			&meal.EstimatedCostCents,
270: 			&consumedInt,
271: 			&meal.ConsumedAt,
272: 		); err != nil {
273: 			return nil, err
274: 		}
275: 		meal.IsConsumed = consumedInt == 1
276: 		meals = append(meals, meal)
277: 	}
278: 
279: 	return meals, rows.Err()
280: }

(End of file - total 280 lines)
</content>

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS meal_plans (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    period_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    start_date DATE NOT NULL,
    end_date DATE NOT NULL,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'accepted',
    source TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ai',
    proposal_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    ai_cost_cents_total INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK (ai_cost_cents_total >= 0),

    updated_at DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    CHECK (status IN ('proposal', 'accepted', 'declined', 'archived')),

);

PROPOSAL2=$(curl -s -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:19094/plans/proposal" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" --data '{"periodType":"day","startDate":"2026-06-08","proposalVersion":2,"aiCostCentsTotal":180,"meals":[{"scheduledDate":"2026-06-08","mealSection":"breakfast","recipeId":"rcp_breakfast_oats","recipeName":"Banana Oat Bowl","servings":1,"estimatedCostCents":180,"macros":{"calories":430,"proteinG":17,"carbsG":69,"fatG":10}}]}'); PLAN2_ID=$(python3 -c 'import json,sys; print(json.loads(sys.argv[1])["plan"]["id"])' "$PROPOSAL2"); DECLINE2=$(curl -s -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:19094/plans/$PLAN2_ID/decline" -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H "Content-Type: application/json" --data '{"reason":"regen please"}'); python3 -c 'import json,sys; proposal=json.loads(sys.argv[1]); accept=json.loads(sys.argv[2]); week=json.loads(sys.argv[3]); decline_after_accept=json.loads(sys.argv[4]); proposal2=json.loads(sys.argv[5]); decline2=json.loads(sys.argv[6]); print(json.dumps({"proposal_status": proposal.get("plan",{}).get("status"), "proposal_days": len(proposal.get("days",[])), "proposal_week_totals": proposal.get("weekTotals"), "accepted_status": accept.get("plan",{}).get("status"), "week_plan_count": len(week.get("plans",[])), "week_days": len(week.get("days",[])), "week_day1_totals": (week.get("days") or [{}])[0].get("totals"), "week_totals": week.get("weekTotals"), "decline_after_accept_error": decline_after_accept.get("error"), "declined_status": decline2.get("plan",{}).get("status"), "declined_notes": decline2.get("plan",{}).get("notes")}, indent=2))' "$PROPOSAL" "$ACCEPT" "$WEEK" "$DECLINE_AFTER_ACCEPT" "$PROPOSAL2" "$DECLINE2"

parts.text:
Not fully. **Step 4 is only partially implemented and currently fails its main response contract.**

Findings:
- `GET /plans/week?start=` does not return meal data in the 7-day calendar payload. In live testing, `days` came back with empty `sections` for every day even though `plan_meals` rows were persisted and weekly totals were non-zero. This breaks the required weekly calendar payload from `tasks/backend.md:66-70`.
- `POST /plans/proposal` and `POST /plans/:id/accept` return `days: null` and zeroed `weekTotals` in live responses, despite persisted meals existing. That means the required macro totals in responses are not actually working for proposal/accept flows.
- Day-level macro totals are missing in practice. `GET /plans/week` returned zero totals for each day, even while `weekTotals` was correctly aggregated. This means the “per day” part of `tasks/backend.md:67-70` is not satisfied.
- Per-meal macro data is not exposed in the weekly response because no meals are attached to day sections. The underlying data exists, but the API contract is not delivering it.

What is implemented:
- Routes exist in `backend/internal/transport/http/router/router.go:40-43`.
- Handler/service/repository stack exists in `backend/internal/transport/http/handlers/plan_handler.go:20`, `backend/internal/services/plan_service.go:34`, and `backend/internal/repositories/plan_repository.go:51`.
- Proposal create, accept, decline, and accepted-plan lookup all execute and persist data.
- Weekly aggregate `weekTotals` is computed correctly for accepted plans.
- Decline lifecycle behavior works: declining a proposal sets status to `declined`, and declining after accept correctly returns conflict.

Likely root cause:
- The response builder is indexing meals by `scheduled_date` strings like `2026-06-01`, but plan summaries appear to be coming back from SQLite as RFC3339-style strings like `2026-06-01T00:00:00Z`. That mismatch likely prevents meals from being attached to the generated day keys in `backend/internal/services/plan_service.go:150-223`.

Verdict:
- `POST /plans/proposal`: partially implemented, response incorrect
- `POST /plans/:id/accept`: partially implemented, response incorrect
- `POST /plans/:id/decline`: implemented
- `GET /plans/week?start=`: partially implemented, missing day sections and day totals
- Macro totals:
  - per meal: not delivered in weekly response
  - per day: broken
  - per week: working

So **step 4 should not be marked complete yet**.
verify that the issues you mentioned are resolved

**Verifying current code**

I need to verify the current code, but I can't use Git for this process. My plan is to inspect the files and run a smoke test. Since this involves multiple steps, I'll use a to-do list to keep track. Should I mention previewing changes? Hmm, actually, it seems there are no changes made, so I’ll focus on providing comments first. Let’s search for the relevant files to get started!

27: 
28: type StoredPlanMeal struct {
29: 	ID                 string
30: 	RecipeID           string
31: 	RecipeName         string
32: 	ScheduledDate      string
33: 	MealSection        string
34: 	Servings           float64
35: 	EstimatedCostCents int
36: 	Calories           float64
37: 	ProteinG           float64
38: 	CarbsG             float64
39: 	FatG               float64
40: 	IsConsumed         bool
41: 	ConsumedAt         string
42: }
43: 
44: type PlanRepository struct {
45: 	db *sql.DB
46: }
47: 
48: func NewPlanRepository(db *sql.DB) *PlanRepository {
49: 	return &PlanRepository{db: db}
50: }
51: 
52: func (r *PlanRepository) CreateProposal(ctx context.Context, userID string, req dto.PlanProposalRequest) (StoredPlan, error) {
53: 	planID, err := id.New("pln")
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
56: 	}
57: 
58: 	tx, err := r.db.BeginTx(ctx, nil)
59: 	if err != nil {
60: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
61: 	}
62: 	defer tx.Rollback()
63: 
64: 	_, err = tx.ExecContext(
65: 		ctx,
66: 		`INSERT INTO meal_plans (id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, notes)
67: 		 VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'proposal', ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
68: 		planID,
69: 		userID,
70: 		req.PeriodType,
71: 		req.StartDate,
72: 		req.EndDate,
73: 		req.Source,
74: 		req.ProposalVersion,
75: 		req.AICostCentsTotal,
76: 		req.Notes,
77: 	)
78: 	if err != nil {
79: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	for _, meal := range req.Meals {
83: 		mealID, err := id.New("pm")
84: 		if err != nil {
85: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
86: 		}
87: 
88: 		_, err = tx.ExecContext(
89: 			ctx,
90: 			`INSERT INTO plan_meals (
91: 				id, meal_plan_id, recipe_id, scheduled_date, meal_section, recipe_name,
92: 				servings, kcal, protein_g, carbs_g, fat_g, estimated_cost_cents
93: 			 ) VALUES (?, ?, NULLIF(?, ''), ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
94: 			mealID,
95: 			planID,
96: 			meal.RecipeID,
97: 			meal.ScheduledDate,
98: 			meal.MealSection,
99: 			meal.RecipeName,
100: 			meal.Servings,
101: 			meal.Macros.Calories,
102: 			meal.Macros.ProteinG,
103: 			meal.Macros.CarbsG,
104: 			meal.Macros.FatG,
105: 			meal.EstimatedCostCents,
106: 		)
107: 		if err != nil {
108: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
109: 		}
110: 	}
111: 
112: 	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
113: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
114: 	}
115: 
116: 	return r.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
117: }
118: 
119: func (r *PlanRepository) GetPlanByID(ctx context.Context, userID, planID string) (StoredPlan, error) {
120: 	var plan StoredPlan
121: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
122: 		ctx,
123: 		`SELECT id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, COALESCE(notes, '')
124: 		 FROM meal_plans
125: 		 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
126: 		planID,
127: 		userID,
128: 	).Scan(
129: 		&plan.ID,
130: 		&plan.UserID,
131: 		&plan.PeriodType,
132: 		&plan.StartDate,
133: 		&plan.EndDate,
134: 		&plan.Status,
135: 		&plan.Source,
136: 		&plan.ProposalVersion,
137: 		&plan.AICostCentsTotal,
138: 		&plan.Notes,
139: 	)
140: 	if err != nil {
141: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
142: 	}
143: 	plan.StartDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.StartDate)
144: 	plan.EndDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.EndDate)
145: 
146: 	meals, err := r.listMealsForPlan(ctx, planID)
147: 	if err != nil {
148: 		return StoredPlan{}, err
149: 	}
150: 	plan.Meals = meals
151: 	return plan, nil
152: }
153: 
154: func (r *PlanRepository) UpdateStatus(ctx context.Context, userID, planID, status, noteSuffix string) (StoredPlan, error) {
155: 	if strings.TrimSpace(noteSuffix) == "" {
156: 		_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
157: 			ctx,
158: 			`UPDATE meal_plans SET status = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
159: 			status,
160: 			planID,
161: 			userID,
162: 		)
163: 		if err != nil {
164: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
165: 		}
166: 	} else {
167: 		_, err := r.db.ExecContext(
168: 			ctx,
169: 			`UPDATE meal_plans
170: 			 SET status = ?,
171: 			     notes = TRIM(COALESCE(notes, '') || CASE WHEN COALESCE(notes, '') = '' THEN '' ELSE ' | ' END || ?),
172: 			     updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
173: 			 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
174: 			status,
175: 			noteSuffix,
176: 			planID,
177: 			userID,
178: 		)
179: 		if err != nil {
180: 			return StoredPlan{}, err
181: 		}
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	return r.GetPlanByID(ctx, userID, planID)
185: }
186: 
187: func (r *PlanRepository) ListAcceptedPlansForRange(ctx context.Context, userID, startDate, endDate string) ([]StoredPlan, error) {
188: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
189: 		ctx,
190: 		`SELECT id, user_id, period_type, start_date, end_date, status, source, proposal_version, ai_cost_cents_total, COALESCE(notes, '')
191: 		 FROM meal_plans
192: 		 WHERE user_id = ? AND status = 'accepted' AND start_date <= ? AND end_date >= ?
193: 		 ORDER BY start_date ASC, created_at ASC`,
194: 		userID,
195: 		endDate,
196: 		startDate,
197: 	)
198: 	if err != nil {
199: 		return nil, err
200: 	}
201: 	defer rows.Close()
202: 
203: 	plans := make([]StoredPlan, 0)
204: 	for rows.Next() {
205: 		var plan StoredPlan
206: 		if err := rows.Scan(
207: 			&plan.ID,
208: 			&plan.UserID,
209: 			&plan.PeriodType,
210: 			&plan.StartDate,
211: 			&plan.EndDate,
212: 			&plan.Status,
213: 			&plan.Source,
214: 			&plan.ProposalVersion,
215: 			&plan.AICostCentsTotal,
216: 			&plan.Notes,
217: 		); err != nil {
218: 			return nil, err
219: 		}
220: 		plan.StartDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.StartDate)
221: 		plan.EndDate = normalizeDateOnly(plan.EndDate)
222: 		plans = append(plans, plan)
223: 	}
224: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
225: 		return nil, err
226: 	}
227: 
228: 	for i := range plans {
229: 		meals, err := r.listMealsForPlanInRange(ctx, plans[i].ID, startDate, endDate)
230: 		if err != nil {
231: 			return nil, err
232: 		}
233: 		plans[i].Meals = meals
234: 	}
235: 
236: 	return plans, nil
237: }
238: 
239: func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlan(ctx context.Context, planID string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
240: 	return r.listMeals(ctx, `WHERE meal_plan_id = ?`, planID)
241: }
242: 
243: func (r *PlanRepository) listMealsForPlanInRange(ctx context.Context, planID, startDate, endDate string) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
244: 	return r.listMeals(ctx, `WHERE meal_plan_id = ? AND scheduled_date >= ? AND scheduled_date <= ?`, planID, startDate, endDate)
245: }
246: 
247: func (r *PlanRepository) listMeals(ctx context.Context, whereClause string, args ...any) ([]StoredPlanMeal, error) {
248: 	query := fmt.Sprintf(`SELECT id, COALESCE(recipe_id, ''), recipe_name, scheduled_date, meal_section, servings,
249: 		 COALESCE(kcal, 0), COALESCE(protein_g, 0), COALESCE(carbs_g, 0), COALESCE(fat_g, 0), estimated_cost_cents,
250: 		 is_consumed, COALESCE(consumed_at, '')
251: 		 FROM plan_meals %s ORDER BY scheduled_date ASC, meal_section ASC`, whereClause)
252: 
253: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(ctx, query, args...)
254: 	if err != nil {
255: 		return nil, err
256: 	}
257: 	defer rows.Close()
258: 
259: 	meals := make([]StoredPlanMeal, 0)
260: 	for rows.Next() {
261: 		var meal StoredPlanMeal
262: 		var consumedInt int
263: 		if err := rows.Scan(
264: 			&meal.ID,
265: 			&meal.RecipeID,
266: 			&meal.RecipeName,
267: 			&meal.ScheduledDate,
268: 			&meal.MealSection,
269: 			&meal.Servings,
270: 			&meal.Calories,
271: 			&meal.ProteinG,
272: 			&meal.CarbsG,
273: 			&meal.FatG,
274: 			&meal.EstimatedCostCents,
275: 			&consumedInt,
276: 			&meal.ConsumedAt,
277: 		); err != nil {
278: 			return nil, err
279: 		}
280: 		meal.ScheduledDate = normalizeDateOnly(meal.ScheduledDate)
281: 		meal.IsConsumed = consumedInt == 1
282: 		meals = append(meals, meal)
283: 	}
284: 
285: 	return meals, rows.Err()
286: }
287: 
288: func normalizeDateOnly(value string) string {
289: 	value = strings.TrimSpace(value)
290: 	if value == "" {
291: 		return value
292: 	}
293: 	if len(value) >= 10 && value[4] == '-' && value[7] == '-' {
294: 		return value[:10]
295: 	}
296: 	for _, layout := range []string{time.RFC3339, "2006-01-02 15:04:05Z07:00", "2006-01-02 15:04:05", "2006-01-02"} {
297: 		if t, err := time.Parse(layout, value); err == nil {
298: 			return t.Format("2006-01-02")
299: 		}
300: 	}
301: 	return value
302: }

(End of file - total 302 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Yes. The issues I flagged are now resolved.

What changed:
- The repository now normalizes SQLite date strings to `YYYY-MM-DD` via `normalizeDateOnly(...)` in `backend/internal/repositories/plan_repository.go:288`, and applies it to both plan dates and meal dates.
- The proposal response builder was hardened against empty inputs in `backend/internal/services/plan_service.go:141`.

What I verified live:
- `POST /plans/proposal` now returns:
  - `days` with 7 entries
  - populated day 1 `sections` for `breakfast`, `lunch`, `dinner`, `snacks`
  - non-zero `weekTotals`
- `POST /plans/:id/accept` now returns:
  - status `accepted`
  - `days` populated
  - non-zero `weekTotals`
- `GET /plans/week?start=2026-06-01` now returns:
  - one accepted plan
  - 7-day payload
  - populated day 1 meal sections
  - correct day totals
  - correct week totals
- `POST /plans/:id/decline` after accept still correctly returns conflict.

Smoke test result:
- Day 1 totals: `1840 kcal`, `109 protein`, `219 carbs`, `53 fat`
- Week totals: same values for this one-day-filled week
- `go test ./...` passes

Verdict:
- The previously reported step 4 response issues are fixed.
- Step 4 P0 now looks functionally implemented based on this verification.

package ai

import "pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"

type PlanResponse struct {
	PeriodType      string            `json:"period_type"`
	StartDate       string            `json:"start_date"`
	EndDate         string            `json:"end_date,omitempty"`
	Currency        string            `json:"currency,omitempty"`
	EstimatedTotal  float64           `json:"estimated_total_cost"`
	Days            []DayPlan         `json:"days"`
	Notes           string            `json:"notes,omitempty"`
	RequestMetadata map[string]string `json:"request_metadata,omitempty"`
}

type DayPlan struct {
	Date  string       `json:"date"`
	Meals []MealDetail `json:"meals"`
}

14: )

16: var (
17: 	ErrInvalidAIJSON        = errors.New("ai response is not valid JSON")
18: 	ErrInvalidAISchema      = errors.New("ai response failed schema validation")
19: 	ErrUnsupportedPlanType  = errors.New("period_type must be one of meal, day, week, month")
20: 	ErrInvalidMealType      = errors.New("meal_type must be one of breakfast, lunch, dinner, snack, snacks")
21: 	ErrMissingDays          = errors.New("days must not be empty")
22: 	ErrMissingMeals         = errors.New("day meals must not be empty")
23: 	ErrInvalidDate          = errors.New("dates must use YYYY-MM-DD")
24: 	ErrInvalidServings      = errors.New("servings must be greater than zero")
25: 	ErrInvalidIngredient    = errors.New("ingredients must include name, quantity, and unit")
26: 	ErrInvalidMacros        = errors.New("macros must include non-negative protein, carbs, fat, and calories")
27: 	ErrInvalidEstimatedCost = errors.New("estimated_cost must be zero or positive")
28: )
29: 
30: type ParseError struct {
31: 	Reason string
32: 	Field  string
33: }
34: 
35: func (e ParseError) Error() string {
36: 	if e.Field == "" {
37: 		return e.Reason
38: 	}
39: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", e.Field, e.Reason)
40: }
41: 
42: func (e ParseError) Unwrap() error {
43: 	return ErrInvalidAISchema
44: }
45: 
46: func ParsePlanResponse(raw string) (NormalizedPlan, error) {
47: 	cleaned, repairs := repairRawJSON(raw)
48: 
49: 	var payload PlanResponse
50: 	if err := decodeStrict(cleaned, &payload); err != nil {
51: 		fallback, convertRepairs, convErr := decodeWithNumericRepair(cleaned)
52: 		if convErr != nil {
53: 			return NormalizedPlan{}, fmt.Errorf("%w: %v", ErrInvalidAIJSON, err)
54: 		}
55: 		payload = fallback
56: 		repairs = append(repairs, convertRepairs...)
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	normalized, err := normalizePlan(payload)
60: 	if err != nil {
61: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, err
62: 	}
63: 	normalized.Parse = ParseMetadata{
64: 		UsedRepair:     len(repairs) > 0,
65: 		RepairActions:  repairs,
66: 		ValidationStep: "normalized",
67: 	}
68: 	return normalized, nil
69: }
70: 
71: func decodeStrict(raw string, dest *PlanResponse) error {
72: 	dec := json.NewDecoder(strings.NewReader(raw))
73: 	dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
74: 	return dec.Decode(dest)
75: }
76: 
77: func decodeWithNumericRepair(raw string) (PlanResponse, []string, error) {
78: 	var generic any
79: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(raw), &generic); err != nil {
80: 		return PlanResponse{}, nil, err
81: 	}
82: 	repaired, actions := coerceNumericStrings(generic, "")
83: 	bytes, err := json.Marshal(repaired)
84: 	if err != nil {
85: 		return PlanResponse{}, nil, err
86: 	}
87: 	var payload PlanResponse
88: 	if err := decodeStrict(string(bytes), &payload); err != nil {
89: 		return PlanResponse{}, nil, err
90: 	}
91: 	return payload, actions, nil
92: }
93: 
94: func normalizePlan(payload PlanResponse) (NormalizedPlan, error) {
95: 	periodType := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(payload.PeriodType))
96: 	if periodType == "" {
97: 		periodType = inferPeriodType(payload)
98: 	}
99: 	switch periodType {
100: 	case "meal", "day", "week", "month":
101: 	default:
102: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "period_type", Reason: ErrUnsupportedPlanType.Error()}
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	if len(payload.Days) == 0 {
106: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "days", Reason: ErrMissingDays.Error()}
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	notes := strings.TrimSpace(payload.Notes)
110: 	proposal := dto.PlanProposalRequest{
111: 		PeriodType:      planProposalPeriodType(periodType),
112: 		StartDate:       strings.TrimSpace(payload.StartDate),
113: 		EndDate:         strings.TrimSpace(payload.EndDate),
114: 		Source:          "ai",
115: 		ProposalVersion: 1,
116: 		Notes:           notes,
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	ingredients := make([]NormalizedIngredient, 0)
120: 	totalCostCents := 0
121: 	seenSlots := make(map[string]struct{})
122: 	sortedDays := append([]DayPlan(nil), payload.Days...)
123: 	sort.Slice(sortedDays, func(i, j int) bool {
124: 		return sortedDays[i].Date < sortedDays[j].Date
125: 	})
126: 
127: 	for dayIndex, day := range sortedDays {
128: 		date := strings.TrimSpace(day.Date)
129: 		if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", date); err != nil {
130: 			return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: fmt.Sprintf("days[%d].date", dayIndex), Reason: ErrInvalidDate.Error()}
131: 		}
132: 		if len(day.Meals) == 0 {
133: 			return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: fmt.Sprintf("days[%d].meals", dayIndex), Reason: ErrMissingMeals.Error()}
134: 		}
135: 		for mealIndex, meal := range day.Meals {
136: 			mealField := fmt.Sprintf("days[%d].meals[%d]", dayIndex, mealIndex)
137: 			mealSection, err := normalizeMealType(meal.MealType)
138: 			if err != nil {
139: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".meal_type", Reason: err.Error()}
140: 			}
141: 			if strings.TrimSpace(meal.RecipeName) == "" {
142: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".recipe_name", Reason: "recipe_name is required"}
143: 			}
144: 			if meal.Servings <= 0 {
145: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".servings", Reason: ErrInvalidServings.Error()}
146: 			}
147: 			if err := validateMacros(mealField+".macros", meal.Macros); err != nil {
148: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, err
149: 			}
150: 			if meal.EstimatedCost < 0 {
151: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".estimated_cost", Reason: ErrInvalidEstimatedCost.Error()}
152: 			}
153: 			if len(meal.Ingredients) == 0 {
154: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".ingredients", Reason: "ingredients must not be empty"}
155: 			}
156: 
157: 			slotKey := date + "|" + mealSection
158: 			if _, exists := seenSlots[slotKey]; exists {
159: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".meal_type", Reason: "duplicate meal section for date"}
160: 			}
161: 			seenSlots[slotKey] = struct{}{}
162: 
163: 			for ingredientIndex, ingredient := range meal.Ingredients {
164: 				name := strings.TrimSpace(ingredient.Name)
165: 				unit := strings.TrimSpace(ingredient.Unit)
166: 				if name == "" || unit == "" || ingredient.Quantity <= 0 {
167: 					return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: fmt.Sprintf("%s.ingredients[%d]", mealField, ingredientIndex), Reason: ErrInvalidIngredient.Error()}
168: 				}
169: 				ingredients = append(ingredients, NormalizedIngredient{
170: 					ScheduledDate: date,
171: 					MealSection:   mealSection,
172: 					RecipeName:    strings.TrimSpace(meal.RecipeName),
173: 					Name:          name,
174: 					Quantity:      ingredient.Quantity,
175: 					Unit:          unit,
176: 				})
177: 			}
178: 
179: 			costCents := dollarsToCents(meal.EstimatedCost)
180: 			totalCostCents += costCents
181: 			proposal.Meals = append(proposal.Meals, dto.PlanMealInput{
182: 				ScheduledDate:      date,
183: 				MealSection:        mealSection,
184: 				RecipeName:         strings.TrimSpace(meal.RecipeName),
185: 				Servings:           meal.Servings,
186: 				EstimatedCostCents: costCents,
187: 				Macros: dto.RecipeMacrosResponse{
188: 					Calories: meal.Macros.Calories,
189: 					ProteinG: meal.Macros.Protein,
190: 					CarbsG:   meal.Macros.Carbs,
191: 					FatG:     meal.Macros.Fat,
192: 				},
193: 			})
194: 		}
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	if proposal.StartDate == "" {
198: 		proposal.StartDate = sortedDays[0].Date
199: 	}
200: 	if proposal.EndDate == "" {
201: 		proposal.EndDate = sortedDays[len(sortedDays)-1].Date
202: 	}
203: 	if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", proposal.StartDate); err != nil {
204: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "start_date", Reason: ErrInvalidDate.Error()}
205: 	}
206: 	if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", proposal.EndDate); err != nil {
207: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "end_date", Reason: ErrInvalidDate.Error()}
208: 	}
209: 	if proposal.EndDate < proposal.StartDate {
210: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "end_date", Reason: "end_date must not be earlier than start_date"}
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	proposal.AICostCentsTotal = totalCostCents
214: 	return NormalizedPlan{Proposal: proposal, Ingredients: ingredients}, nil
215: }
216: 
217: func validateMacros(field string, macros MacroInput) error {
218: 	if macros.Protein < 0 || macros.Carbs < 0 || macros.Fat < 0 || macros.Calories < 0 {
219: 		return ParseError{Field: field, Reason: ErrInvalidMacros.Error()}
220: 	}
221: 	return nil
222: }
223: 
224: func normalizeMealType(raw string) (string, error) {
225: 	mealType := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(raw))
226: 	switch mealType {
227: 	case "breakfast", "lunch", "dinner":
228: 		return mealType, nil
229: 	case "snack", "snacks":
230: 		return "snacks", nil
231: 	default:
232: 		return "", ErrInvalidMealType
233: 	}
234: }
235: 
236: func planProposalPeriodType(periodType string) string {
237: 	if periodType == "meal" {
238: 		return "day"
239: 	}
240: 	return periodType
241: }
242: 
243: func inferPeriodType(payload PlanResponse) string {
244: 	if len(payload.Days) == 1 && len(payload.Days[0].Meals) == 1 {
245: 		return "meal"
246: 	}
247: 	if len(payload.Days) == 1 {
248: 		return "day"
249: 	}
250: 	if len(payload.Days) <= 7 {
251: 		return "week"
252: 	}
253: 	return "month"
254: }
255: 
256: func dollarsToCents(value float64) int {
257: 	return int(math.Round(value * 100))
258: }
259: 
260: func repairRawJSON(raw string) (string, []string) {
261: 	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
262: 	actions := make([]string, 0)
263: 	if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "```") {
264: 		trimmed = strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "```json")
265: 		trimmed = strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "```")
266: 		trimmed = strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(trimmed), "```")
267: 		actions = append(actions, "stripped_markdown_fences")
268: 	}
269: 	return strings.TrimSpace(trimmed), actions
270: }
271: 
272: func coerceNumericStrings(value any, path string) (any, []string) {
273: 	actions := make([]string, 0)
274: 	switch typed := value.(type) {
275: 	case map[string]any:
276: 		result := make(map[string]any, len(typed))
277: 		for key, item := range typed {
278: 			nextPath := key
279: 			if path != "" {
280: 				nextPath = path + "." + key
281: 			}
282: 			converted, nestedActions := coerceNumericStrings(item, nextPath)
283: 			result[key] = converted
284: 			actions = append(actions, nestedActions...)
285: 		}
286: 		return result, actions
287: 	case []any:
288: 		result := make([]any, len(typed))
289: 		for index, item := range typed {
290: 			nextPath := fmt.Sprintf("%s[%d]", path, index)
291: 			converted, nestedActions := coerceNumericStrings(item, nextPath)
292: 			result[index] = converted
293: 			actions = append(actions, nestedActions...)
294: 		}
295: 		return result, actions
296: 	case string:
297: 		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(typed)
298: 		if trimmed == "" {
299: 			return typed, nil
300: 		}
301: 		if looksNumericPath(path) {
302: 			if n, err := strconv.ParseFloat(trimmed, 64); err == nil {
303: 				actions = append(actions, "coerced_numeric_string:"+path)
304: 				return n, actions
305: 			}
306: 		}
307: 		return typed, nil
308: 	default:
309: 		return value, nil
310: 	}
311: }
312: 
313: func looksNumericPath(path string) bool {
314: 	for _, suffix := range []string{
315: 		"servings",
316: 		"estimated_cost",
317: 		"estimated_total_cost",
318: 		"quantity",
319: 		"protein",
320: 		"carbs",
321: 		"fat",
322: 		"calories",
323: 	} {
324: 		if strings.HasSuffix(path, suffix) {
325: 			return true
326: 		}
327: 	}
328: 	return false
329: }

(End of file - total 329 lines)
</content>

1: package ai
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bytes"
5: 	"context"
6: 	"encoding/json"
7: 	"errors"
8: 	"fmt"
9: 	"io"
10: 	"net"
11: 	"net/http"
12: 	"net/url"
13: 	"strings"
14: 	"time"
15: 
16: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/config"
17: )
18: 
19: var (
20: 	ErrMissingAPIKey      = errors.New("gemini api key is not configured")
21: 	ErrEmptyPrompt        = errors.New("gemini prompt must not be empty")
22: 	ErrUnexpectedResponse = errors.New("gemini returned no text response")
23: )
24: 
25: type Client struct {
26: 	apiKey         string
27: 	model          string
28: 	baseURL        string
29: 	timeout        time.Duration
30: 	retryMax       int
31: 	retryBackoff   time.Duration
32: 	responseFormat string
33: 	httpClient     *http.Client
34: }
35: 
36: type GenerateRequest struct {
37: 	Prompt           string
38: 	ResponseMIMEType string
39: 	Temperature      *float64
40: }
41: 
42: type GenerateResponse struct {
43: 	Text       string
44: 	Model      string
45: 	Latency    time.Duration
46: 	StatusCode int
47: 	RequestID  string
48: }
49: 
50: type geminiGenerateContentRequest struct {
51: 	Contents          []geminiContent          `json:"contents"`
52: 	GenerationConfig  geminiGenerationConfig   `json:"generationConfig,omitempty"`
53: 	SystemInstruction *geminiSystemInstruction `json:"systemInstruction,omitempty"`
54: }
55: 
56: type geminiContent struct {
57: 	Parts []geminiPart `json:"parts"`
58: }
59: 
60: type geminiPart struct {
61: 	Text string `json:"text"`
62: }
63: 
64: type geminiSystemInstruction struct {
65: 	Parts []geminiPart `json:"parts"`
66: }
67: 
68: type geminiGenerationConfig struct {
69: 	CandidateCount   int      `json:"candidateCount,omitempty"`
70: 	Temperature      *float64 `json:"temperature,omitempty"`
71: 	ResponseMIMEType string   `json:"responseMimeType,omitempty"`
72: }
73: 
74: type geminiGenerateContentResponse struct {
75: 	Candidates []struct {
76: 		Content struct {
77: 			Parts []struct {
78: 				Text string `json:"text"`
79: 			} `json:"parts"`
80: 		} `json:"content"`
81: 	} `json:"candidates"`
82: 	UsageMetadata struct {
83: 		PromptTokenCount     int `json:"promptTokenCount"`
84: 		CandidatesTokenCount int `json:"candidatesTokenCount"`
85: 		TotalTokenCount      int `json:"totalTokenCount"`
86: 	} `json:"usageMetadata"`
87: }
88: 
89: type Config struct {
90: 	APIKey         string
91: 	Model          string
92: 	BaseURL        string
93: 	Timeout        time.Duration
94: 	RetryMax       int
95: 	RetryBackoff   time.Duration
96: 	ResponseFormat string
97: }
98: 
99: func ConfigFromApp(cfg config.Config) Config {
100: 	return Config{
101: 		APIKey:         cfg.GeminiAPIKey,
102: 		Model:          cfg.GeminiModel,
103: 		BaseURL:        cfg.GeminiBaseURL,
104: 		Timeout:        cfg.GeminiTimeout,
105: 		RetryMax:       cfg.GeminiRetryMax,
106: 		RetryBackoff:   cfg.GeminiRetryBackoff,
107: 		ResponseFormat: cfg.GeminiResponseFormat,
108: 	}
109: }
110: 
111: func NewClient(cfg Config) (*Client, error) {
112: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.APIKey) == "" {
113: 		return nil, ErrMissingAPIKey
114: 	}
115: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.Model) == "" {
116: 		cfg.Model = "gemini-2.0-flash"
117: 	}
118: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.BaseURL) == "" {
119: 		cfg.BaseURL = "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
120: 	}
121: 	if cfg.Timeout <= 0 {
122: 		cfg.Timeout = 20 * time.Second
123: 	}
124: 	if cfg.RetryMax < 0 {
125: 		cfg.RetryMax = 0
126: 	}
127: 	if cfg.RetryBackoff <= 0 {
128: 		cfg.RetryBackoff = 500 * time.Millisecond
129: 	}
130: 	if strings.TrimSpace(cfg.ResponseFormat) == "" {
131: 		cfg.ResponseFormat = "application/json"
132: 	}
133: 
134: 	transport := http.DefaultTransport.(*http.Transport).Clone()
135: 	transport.ResponseHeaderTimeout = cfg.Timeout
136: 	transport.DialContext = (&net.Dialer{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}).DialContext
137: 
138: 	return &Client{
139: 		apiKey:         cfg.APIKey,
140: 		model:          cfg.Model,
141: 		baseURL:        strings.TrimRight(cfg.BaseURL, "/"),
142: 		timeout:        cfg.Timeout,
143: 		retryMax:       cfg.RetryMax,
144: 		retryBackoff:   cfg.RetryBackoff,
145: 		responseFormat: cfg.ResponseFormat,
146: 		httpClient: &http.Client{
147: 			Timeout:   cfg.Timeout,
148: 			Transport: transport,
149: 		},
150: 	}, nil
151: }
152: 
153: func (c *Client) Generate(ctx context.Context, req GenerateRequest) (GenerateResponse, error) {
154: 	prompt := strings.TrimSpace(req.Prompt)
155: 	if prompt == "" {
156: 		return GenerateResponse{}, ErrEmptyPrompt
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	requestBody := geminiGenerateContentRequest{
160: 		Contents: []geminiContent{{
161: 			Parts: []geminiPart{{Text: prompt}},
162: 		}},
163: 		SystemInstruction: &geminiSystemInstruction{
164: 			Parts: []geminiPart{{Text: "Return only the requested content. Do not wrap JSON in markdown fences."}},
165: 		},
166: 		GenerationConfig: geminiGenerationConfig{
167: 			CandidateCount:   1,
168: 			Temperature:      req.Temperature,
169: 			ResponseMIMEType: c.responseMIMEType(req),
170: 		},
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	body, err := json.Marshal(requestBody)
174: 	if err != nil {
175: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
176: 	}
177: 
178: 	endpoint := fmt.Sprintf("%s/v1beta/models/%s:generateContent?key=%s", c.baseURL, url.PathEscape(c.model), url.QueryEscape(c.apiKey))
179: 
180: 	var lastErr error
181: 	for attempt := 0; attempt <= c.retryMax; attempt++ {
182: 		start := time.Now()
183: 		response, err := c.doRequest(ctx, endpoint, body)
184: 		if err == nil {
185: 			response.Latency = time.Since(start)
186: 			return response, nil
187: 		}
188: 		lastErr = err
189: 		if attempt == c.retryMax || !isRetryable(err) {
190: 			break
191: 		}
192: 
193: 		select {
194: 		case <-ctx.Done():
195: 			return GenerateResponse{}, ctx.Err()
196: 		case <-time.After(c.retryBackoff * time.Duration(attempt+1)):
197: 		}
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	return GenerateResponse{}, lastErr
201: }
202: 
203: func (c *Client) doRequest(ctx context.Context, endpoint string, body []byte) (GenerateResponse, error) {
204: 	req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, endpoint, bytes.NewReader(body))
205: 	if err != nil {
206: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
207: 	}
208: 	req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
209: 
210: 	resp, err := c.httpClient.Do(req)
211: 	if err != nil {
212: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
213: 	}
214: 	defer resp.Body.Close()
215: 
216: 	responseBody, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
217: 	if err != nil {
218: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	requestID := strings.TrimSpace(resp.Header.Get("x-request-id"))
222: 	if requestID == "" {
223: 		requestID = strings.TrimSpace(resp.Header.Get("x-goog-request-id"))
224: 	}
225: 
226: 	if resp.StatusCode >= http.StatusBadRequest {
227: 		return GenerateResponse{}, httpStatusError{
228: 			StatusCode: resp.StatusCode,
229: 			Body:       string(responseBody),
230: 			RequestID:  requestID,
231: 		}
232: 	}
233: 
234: 	var parsed geminiGenerateContentResponse
235: 	if err := json.Unmarshal(responseBody, &parsed); err != nil {
236: 		return GenerateResponse{}, err
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	text := extractResponseText(parsed)
240: 	if text == "" {
241: 		return GenerateResponse{}, ErrUnexpectedResponse
242: 	}
243: 
244: 	return GenerateResponse{
245: 		Text:       text,
246: 		Model:      c.model,
247: 		StatusCode: resp.StatusCode,
248: 		RequestID:  requestID,
249: 	}, nil
250: }
251: 
252: func (c *Client) responseMIMEType(req GenerateRequest) string {
253: 	if strings.TrimSpace(req.ResponseMIMEType) != "" {
254: 		return req.ResponseMIMEType
255: 	}
256: 	return c.responseFormat
257: }
258: 
259: func extractResponseText(resp geminiGenerateContentResponse) string {
260: 	parts := make([]string, 0)
261: 	for _, candidate := range resp.Candidates {
262: 		for _, part := range candidate.Content.Parts {
263: 			text := strings.TrimSpace(part.Text)
264: 			if text != "" {
265: 				parts = append(parts, text)
266: 			}
267: 		}
268: 	}
269: 	return strings.TrimSpace(strings.Join(parts, "\n"))
270: }
271: 
272: func isRetryable(err error) bool {
273: 	var statusErr httpStatusError
274: 	if errors.As(err, &statusErr) {
275: 		return statusErr.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests || statusErr.StatusCode >= http.StatusInternalServerError
276: 	}
277: 
278: 	var netErr net.Error
279: 	return errors.As(err, &netErr)
280: }
281: 
282: type httpStatusError struct {
283: 	StatusCode int
284: 	Body       string
285: 	RequestID  string
286: }
287: 
288: func (e httpStatusError) Error() string {
289: 	if e.RequestID != "" {
290: 		return fmt.Sprintf("gemini request failed with status %d (request_id=%s): %s", e.StatusCode, e.RequestID, strings.TrimSpace(e.Body))
291: 	}
292: 	return fmt.Sprintf("gemini request failed with status %d: %s", e.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(e.Body))
293: }

(End of file - total 293 lines)
</content>

parts.text:
Mostly yes. **`tasks/ai.md` step 2 is implemented correctly in the backend AI module**, with one naming mismatch to be aware of.

What is implemented:
- **Strict JSON schema definition** exists in `backend/internal/modules/ai/schema.go:5-42`.
- **Required meal fields** are present in the schema:
  - `meal_type` at `schema.go:22`
  - `recipe_name` at `schema.go:23`
  - ingredient list with quantity/unit at `schema.go:25`, `schema.go:31-35`
  - macros at `schema.go:26`, `schema.go:37-42`
  - estimated cost at `schema.go:27`
- **Validation/parsing layer with clear error reasons** exists in `backend/internal/modules/ai/parser.go:46-68` and field-specific `ParseError` handling at `parser.go:30-44`.
- **Reject malformed responses before persistence** is implemented:
  - strict decode with `DisallowUnknownFields()` at `parser.go:71-75`
  - schema validation with precise field paths at `parser.go:94-215`
  - invalid outputs return typed errors like `ErrInvalidAISchema`, `ErrInvalidAIJSON`, `ErrInvalidMealType`, etc.
- **Repair malformed responses before persistence** is also implemented:
  - strips markdown fences at `parser.go:260-270`
  - coerces numeric strings for known numeric fields at `parser.go:272-329`
  - tracks repair metadata in `ParseMetadata` at `schema.go:60-64`

Verification:
- `go test ./internal/modules/ai/...` passes.
- Full backend test suite passes.
- The parser tests cover:
  - valid structured output: `backend/internal/modules/ai/parser_test.go:9`
  - repaired output from fenced JSON + numeric strings: `parser_test.go:64`
  - invalid meal type rejection: `parser_test.go:106`
  - unknown field rejection: `parser_test.go:141`

Important caveat:
- The task says required field `meal_type` should be one of `breakfast/lunch/dinner/snack`, but the parser normalizes `snack` to backend section `snacks` in `parser.go:224-233`. That is intentional and likely correct for backend compatibility, but it means the AI schema and backend schema are not using exactly the same enum label.

Verdict:
- **Step 2 P0 is implemented**
- The implementation is solid and tested
- Only minor note: `snack` is normalized to `snacks`, so that contract should be documented clearly

## Constraints & Preferences
- Run local DB bootstrap/reset via scripts: ./scripts/db/reset-local-db or ./scripts/db/local_db.sh [setup|reset].

package ai

import "pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"

type PlanResponse struct {
	PeriodType      string            `json:"period_type"`
	StartDate       string            `json:"start_date"`
	EndDate         string            `json:"end_date,omitempty"`
	Currency        string            `json:"currency,omitempty"`
	EstimatedTotal  float64           `json:"estimated_total_cost"`
	Days            []DayPlan         `json:"days"`
	Notes           string            `json:"notes,omitempty"`
	RequestMetadata map[string]string `json:"request_metadata,omitempty"`
}

type DayPlan struct {
	Date  string       `json:"date"`
	Meals []MealDetail `json:"meals"`
}

1: package ai
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"encoding/json"
5: 	"errors"
6: 	"fmt"
7: 	"math"
8: 	"sort"
9: 	"strconv"
10: 	"strings"
11: 	"time"
12: 
13: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
14: )
15: 
16: var (
17: 	ErrInvalidAIJSON        = errors.New("ai response is not valid JSON")
18: 	ErrInvalidAISchema      = errors.New("ai response failed schema validation")
19: 	ErrUnsupportedPlanType  = errors.New("period_type must be one of meal, day, week, month")
20: 	ErrInvalidMealType      = errors.New("meal_type must be one of breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks (the alias snack is also accepted)")
21: 	ErrMissingDays          = errors.New("days must not be empty")
22: 	ErrMissingMeals         = errors.New("day meals must not be empty")
23: 	ErrInvalidDate          = errors.New("dates must use YYYY-MM-DD")
24: 	ErrInvalidServings      = errors.New("servings must be greater than zero")
25: 	ErrInvalidIngredient    = errors.New("ingredients must include name, quantity, and unit")
26: 	ErrInvalidMacros        = errors.New("macros must include non-negative protein, carbs, fat, and calories")
27: 	ErrInvalidEstimatedCost = errors.New("estimated_cost must be zero or positive")
28: )
29: 
30: type ParseError struct {
31: 	Reason string
32: 	Field  string
33: }
34: 
35: func (e ParseError) Error() string {
36: 	if e.Field == "" {
37: 		return e.Reason
38: 	}
39: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s: %s", e.Field, e.Reason)
40: }
41: 
42: func (e ParseError) Unwrap() error {
43: 	return ErrInvalidAISchema
44: }
45: 
46: func ParsePlanResponse(raw string) (NormalizedPlan, error) {
47: 	cleaned, repairs := repairRawJSON(raw)
48: 
49: 	var payload PlanResponse
50: 	if err := decodeStrict(cleaned, &payload); err != nil {
51: 		fallback, convertRepairs, convErr := decodeWithNumericRepair(cleaned)
52: 		if convErr != nil {
53: 			return NormalizedPlan{}, fmt.Errorf("%w: %v", ErrInvalidAIJSON, err)
54: 		}
55: 		payload = fallback
56: 		repairs = append(repairs, convertRepairs...)
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	normalized, err := normalizePlan(payload)
60: 	if err != nil {
61: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, err
62: 	}
63: 	normalized.Parse = ParseMetadata{
64: 		UsedRepair:     len(repairs) > 0,
65: 		RepairActions:  repairs,
66: 		ValidationStep: "normalized",
67: 	}
68: 	return normalized, nil
69: }
70: 
71: func decodeStrict(raw string, dest *PlanResponse) error {
72: 	dec := json.NewDecoder(strings.NewReader(raw))
73: 	dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
74: 	return dec.Decode(dest)
75: }
76: 
77: func decodeWithNumericRepair(raw string) (PlanResponse, []string, error) {
78: 	var generic any
79: 	if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(raw), &generic); err != nil {
80: 		return PlanResponse{}, nil, err
81: 	}
82: 	repaired, actions := coerceNumericStrings(generic, "")
83: 	bytes, err := json.Marshal(repaired)
84: 	if err != nil {
85: 		return PlanResponse{}, nil, err
86: 	}
87: 	var payload PlanResponse
88: 	if err := decodeStrict(string(bytes), &payload); err != nil {
89: 		return PlanResponse{}, nil, err
90: 	}
91: 	return payload, actions, nil
92: }
93: 
94: func normalizePlan(payload PlanResponse) (NormalizedPlan, error) {
95: 	periodType := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(payload.PeriodType))
96: 	if periodType == "" {
97: 		periodType = inferPeriodType(payload)
98: 	}
99: 	switch periodType {
100: 	case "meal", "day", "week", "month":
101: 	default:
102: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "period_type", Reason: ErrUnsupportedPlanType.Error()}
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	if len(payload.Days) == 0 {
106: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "days", Reason: ErrMissingDays.Error()}
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	notes := strings.TrimSpace(payload.Notes)
110: 	proposal := dto.PlanProposalRequest{
111: 		PeriodType:      planProposalPeriodType(periodType),
112: 		StartDate:       strings.TrimSpace(payload.StartDate),
113: 		EndDate:         strings.TrimSpace(payload.EndDate),
114: 		Source:          "ai",
115: 		ProposalVersion: 1,
116: 		Notes:           notes,
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	ingredients := make([]NormalizedIngredient, 0)
120: 	totalCostCents := 0
121: 	seenSlots := make(map[string]struct{})
122: 	sortedDays := append([]DayPlan(nil), payload.Days...)
123: 	sort.Slice(sortedDays, func(i, j int) bool {
124: 		return sortedDays[i].Date < sortedDays[j].Date
125: 	})
126: 
127: 	for dayIndex, day := range sortedDays {
128: 		date := strings.TrimSpace(day.Date)
129: 		if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", date); err != nil {
130: 			return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: fmt.Sprintf("days[%d].date", dayIndex), Reason: ErrInvalidDate.Error()}
131: 		}
132: 		if len(day.Meals) == 0 {
133: 			return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: fmt.Sprintf("days[%d].meals", dayIndex), Reason: ErrMissingMeals.Error()}
134: 		}
135: 		for mealIndex, meal := range day.Meals {
136: 			mealField := fmt.Sprintf("days[%d].meals[%d]", dayIndex, mealIndex)
137: 			mealSection, err := normalizeMealType(meal.MealType)
138: 			if err != nil {
139: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".meal_type", Reason: err.Error()}
140: 			}
141: 			if strings.TrimSpace(meal.RecipeName) == "" {
142: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".recipe_name", Reason: "recipe_name is required"}
143: 			}
144: 			if meal.Servings <= 0 {
145: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".servings", Reason: ErrInvalidServings.Error()}
146: 			}
147: 			if err := validateMacros(mealField+".macros", meal.Macros); err != nil {
148: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, err
149: 			}
150: 			if meal.EstimatedCost < 0 {
151: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".estimated_cost", Reason: ErrInvalidEstimatedCost.Error()}
152: 			}
153: 			if len(meal.Ingredients) == 0 {
154: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".ingredients", Reason: "ingredients must not be empty"}
155: 			}
156: 
157: 			slotKey := date + "|" + mealSection
158: 			if _, exists := seenSlots[slotKey]; exists {
159: 				return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: mealField + ".meal_type", Reason: "duplicate meal section for date"}
160: 			}
161: 			seenSlots[slotKey] = struct{}{}
162: 
163: 			for ingredientIndex, ingredient := range meal.Ingredients {
164: 				name := strings.TrimSpace(ingredient.Name)
165: 				unit := strings.TrimSpace(ingredient.Unit)
166: 				if name == "" || unit == "" || ingredient.Quantity <= 0 {
167: 					return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: fmt.Sprintf("%s.ingredients[%d]", mealField, ingredientIndex), Reason: ErrInvalidIngredient.Error()}
168: 				}
169: 				ingredients = append(ingredients, NormalizedIngredient{
170: 					ScheduledDate: date,
171: 					MealSection:   mealSection,
172: 					RecipeName:    strings.TrimSpace(meal.RecipeName),
173: 					Name:          name,
174: 					Quantity:      ingredient.Quantity,
175: 					Unit:          unit,
176: 				})
177: 			}
178: 
179: 			costCents := dollarsToCents(meal.EstimatedCost)
180: 			totalCostCents += costCents
181: 			proposal.Meals = append(proposal.Meals, dto.PlanMealInput{
182: 				ScheduledDate:      date,
183: 				MealSection:        mealSection,
184: 				RecipeName:         strings.TrimSpace(meal.RecipeName),
185: 				Servings:           meal.Servings,
186: 				EstimatedCostCents: costCents,
187: 				Macros: dto.RecipeMacrosResponse{
188: 					Calories: meal.Macros.Calories,
189: 					ProteinG: meal.Macros.Protein,
190: 					CarbsG:   meal.Macros.Carbs,
191: 					FatG:     meal.Macros.Fat,
192: 				},
193: 			})
194: 		}
195: 	}
196: 
197: 	if proposal.StartDate == "" {
198: 		proposal.StartDate = sortedDays[0].Date
199: 	}
200: 	if proposal.EndDate == "" {
201: 		proposal.EndDate = sortedDays[len(sortedDays)-1].Date
202: 	}
203: 	if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", proposal.StartDate); err != nil {
204: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "start_date", Reason: ErrInvalidDate.Error()}
205: 	}
206: 	if _, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", proposal.EndDate); err != nil {
207: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "end_date", Reason: ErrInvalidDate.Error()}
208: 	}
209: 	if proposal.EndDate < proposal.StartDate {
210: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, ParseError{Field: "end_date", Reason: "end_date must not be earlier than start_date"}
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	proposal.AICostCentsTotal = totalCostCents
214: 	return NormalizedPlan{Proposal: proposal, Ingredients: ingredients}, nil
215: }
216: 
217: func validateMacros(field string, macros MacroInput) error {
218: 	if macros.Protein < 0 || macros.Carbs < 0 || macros.Fat < 0 || macros.Calories < 0 {
219: 		return ParseError{Field: field, Reason: ErrInvalidMacros.Error()}
220: 	}
221: 	return nil
222: }
223: 
224: func normalizeMealType(raw string) (string, error) {
225: 	mealType := strings.TrimSpace(strings.ToLower(raw))
226: 	switch mealType {
227: 	case "breakfast", "lunch", "dinner":
228: 		return mealType, nil
229: 	case "snack", "snacks":
230: 		// Keep one canonical downstream value while tolerating the singular alias from model output.
231: 		return "snacks", nil
232: 	default:
233: 		return "", ErrInvalidMealType
234: 	}
235: }
236: 
237: func planProposalPeriodType(periodType string) string {
238: 	if periodType == "meal" {
239: 		return "day"
240: 	}
241: 	return periodType
242: }
243: 
244: func inferPeriodType(payload PlanResponse) string {
245: 	if len(payload.Days) == 1 && len(payload.Days[0].Meals) == 1 {
246: 		return "meal"
247: 	}
248: 	if len(payload.Days) == 1 {
249: 		return "day"
250: 	}
251: 	if len(payload.Days) <= 7 {
252: 		return "week"
253: 	}
254: 	return "month"
255: }
256: 
257: func dollarsToCents(value float64) int {
258: 	return int(math.Round(value * 100))
259: }
260: 
261: func repairRawJSON(raw string) (string, []string) {
262: 	trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(raw)
263: 	actions := make([]string, 0)
264: 	if strings.HasPrefix(trimmed, "```") {
265: 		trimmed = strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "```json")
266: 		trimmed = strings.TrimPrefix(trimmed, "```")
267: 		trimmed = strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(trimmed), "```")
268: 		actions = append(actions, "stripped_markdown_fences")
269: 	}
270: 	return strings.TrimSpace(trimmed), actions
271: }
272: 
273: func coerceNumericStrings(value any, path string) (any, []string) {
274: 	actions := make([]string, 0)
275: 	switch typed := value.(type) {
276: 	case map[string]any:
277: 		result := make(map[string]any, len(typed))
278: 		for key, item := range typed {
279: 			nextPath := key
280: 			if path != "" {
281: 				nextPath = path + "." + key
282: 			}
283: 			converted, nestedActions := coerceNumericStrings(item, nextPath)
284: 			result[key] = converted
285: 			actions = append(actions, nestedActions...)
286: 		}
287: 		return result, actions
288: 	case []any:
289: 		result := make([]any, len(typed))
290: 		for index, item := range typed {
291: 			nextPath := fmt.Sprintf("%s[%d]", path, index)
292: 			converted, nestedActions := coerceNumericStrings(item, nextPath)
293: 			result[index] = converted
294: 			actions = append(actions, nestedActions...)
295: 		}
296: 		return result, actions
297: 	case string:
298: 		trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(typed)
299: 		if trimmed == "" {
300: 			return typed, nil
301: 		}
302: 		if looksNumericPath(path) {
303: 			if n, err := strconv.ParseFloat(trimmed, 64); err == nil {
304: 				actions = append(actions, "coerced_numeric_string:"+path)
305: 				return n, actions
306: 			}
307: 		}
308: 		return typed, nil
309: 	default:
310: 		return value, nil
311: 	}
312: }
313: 
314: func looksNumericPath(path string) bool {
315: 	for _, suffix := range []string{
316: 		"servings",
317: 		"estimated_cost",
318: 		"estimated_total_cost",
319: 		"quantity",
320: 		"protein",
321: 		"carbs",
322: 		"fat",
323: 		"calories",
324: 	} {
325: 		if strings.HasSuffix(path, suffix) {
326: 			return true
327: 		}
328: 	}
329: 	return false
330: }

(End of file - total 330 lines)
</content>

32: func BuildPrompt(template PromptTemplate, req PromptRequest) (string, error) {
33: 	var objective string
34: 	switch template {
35: 	case PromptTemplateSingleMeal:
36: 		objective = "Create a single meal recommendation."
37: 	case PromptTemplateDayPlan:
38: 		objective = "Create a 1-day meal plan with exactly 4 sections: breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks."
39: 	case PromptTemplateWeekPlan:

41: 	case PromptTemplateMonthPlan:

45: 	}

52: 		"Use the exact meal_type values breakfast, lunch, dinner, and snacks.",
53: 	}

60: 	}
61: 	if req.StartDate != "" || req.EndDate != "" {
62: 		sections = append(sections, fmt.Sprintf("Plan window: start=%s end=%s", valueOrPlaceholder(req.StartDate), valueOrPlaceholder(req.EndDate)))
63: 	}
64: 	if budget := strings.TrimSpace(req.BudgetTarget); budget != "" {
65: 		sections = append(sections, "Budget target:\n"+budget)
66: 	}

Verdict: Step 2 (Output schema and parser) is implemented correctly.

Evidence mapped to the checklist

- Strict JSON schema for AI output
  - PlanResponse, DayPlan, MealDetail, IngredientInput, MacroInput types defined in backend/internal/modules/ai/schema.go (lines ~5–42).
  - Parser uses json.Decoder with DisallowUnknownFields to enforce strict decoding: backend/internal/modules/ai/parser.go decodeStrict (lines 71–75).

- Required fields per meal enforced
  - MealDetail fields: meal_type, recipe_name, ingredients (with name/quantity/unit), macros (protein/carbs/fat/calories), estimated_cost are present in schema.go (lines ~21–27, 31–41).
  - normalizePlan validates required fields and constraints (non-empty recipe_name, servings > 0, ingredients non-empty, ingredient name/unit/quantity > 0, macros non-negative, estimated_cost >= 0): backend/internal/modules/ai/parser.go (lines ~137–176, 147–152, 163–176).

- Validation / parsing layer with clear error reasons
  - ParsePlanResponse wraps parsing + normalization and returns ParseError with field-specific reasons; ErrInvalidAISchema and other error values are defined (parser.go lines ~16–28, ParseError type lines 30–44, ParsePlanResponse and normalizePlan lines ~46–69 and 94–115 and throughout validation).
  - ParseMetadata is recorded on normalized output (schema.go ParseMetadata lines ~60–63 and parser.go lines 63–67).

- Reject / repair malformed responses before persistence
  - repairRawJSON strips markdown fences (```) and records action "stripped_markdown_fences": parser.go repairRawJSON (lines ~261–271).
  - decodeWithNumericRepair + coerceNumericStrings will coerce numeric strings into numbers for relevant fields (servings, quantity, protein, carbs, fat, calories, estimated_cost, estimated_total_cost) and record actions "coerced_numeric_string:<path>": parser.go decodeWithNumericRepair (77–92), coerceNumericStrings (273–312), looksNumericPath (314–330).
  - Flow: ParsePlanResponse tries strict decode; on strict decode failure it attempts numeric-coercion fallback; repairs are recorded and exposed in ParseMetadata (parser.go lines ~46–57, 63–67).

Tests that validate behaviour
- backend/internal/modules/ai/parser_test.go contains tests exercising:
  - valid week plan parsing and snack -> snacks normalization (lines ~9–62).
  - repairs for markdown fences and numeric-strings and that repair metadata is set (lines ~64–104).
  - rejecting invalid meal types and unknown fields (lines ~106–171).

Minor notes / possible follow-ups (non-blocking)
- decodeStrict returns ErrInvalidAIJSON for unknown fields (this is intentional and covered by tests). If the UX needs unknown-field warnings instead of hard rejection, adjust behavior.
- repairRawJSON removes triple-backtick fences but assumes fenced block starts with exactly "```json" or "```". That covers the common case; if model outputs other wrappers (like code blocks with language variations), consider making fence stripping more robust.
- coerceNumericStrings only coerces fields whose path ends with a known suffix. This is correct and tested, but adding more suffixes or pattern support may catch additional numeric variants.
- Ingredient matching to USDA / unmatched reporting is not part of step 2 (task 4); the parser currently returns NormalizedIngredient list and space for UnmatchedReasons but does not perform DB matching here — that's expected per roadmap.

Recommendation
- From a QA perspective, step 2 is complete and well-covered by unit tests. 1: package services
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"context"
5: 	"database/sql"
6: 	"errors"
7: 	"fmt"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/repositories"
11: 	"pantrypal/backend/internal/transport/http/dto"
12: )
13: 
14: var (
15: 	ErrPlanMealNotFound    = errors.New("plan meal not found")
16: 	ErrMealAlreadyConsumed = errors.New("meal has already been consumed")
17: )
18: 
19: type ConsumeService struct {
20: 	plans    *repositories.PlanRepository
21: 	recipes  *repositories.RecipeRepository
22: 	pantry   *repositories.PantryRepository
23: 	consumption *repositories.ConsumptionLogRepository
24: }
25: 
26: func NewConsumeService(plans *repositories.PlanRepository, recipes *repositories.RecipeRepository, pantry *repositories.PantryRepository, consumption *repositories.ConsumptionLogRepository) *ConsumeService {
27: 	return &ConsumeService{plans: plans, recipes: recipes, pantry: pantry, consumption: consumption}
28: }
29: 
30: func (s *ConsumeService) ConsumeMeal(ctx context.Context, userID, mealID string) (dto.ConsumeMealResponse, error) {
31: 	meal, err := s.plans.GetPlanMealByID(ctx, userID, mealID)
32: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
33: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, ErrPlanMealNotFound
34: 	}
35: 	if err != nil {
36: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, err
37: 	}
38: 
39: 	if meal.IsConsumed {
40: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, ErrMealAlreadyConsumed
41: 	}
42: 
43: 	var items []dto.ConsumedItemInfo
44: 
45: 	if meal.RecipeID != "" {
46: 		recipe, err := s.recipes.GetByID(ctx, meal.RecipeID)
47: 		if err != nil {
48: 			return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("load recipe: %w", err)
49: 		}
50: 
51: 		scale := meal.Servings / float64(recipe.Servings)
52: 
53: 		for _, ing := range recipe.Ingredients {
54: 			deductQty := ing.Quantity * scale
55: 
56: 			info := s.deductIngredient(ctx, userID, mealID, ing.FDCID, ing.Description, deductQty, ing.Unit)
57: 			items = append(items, info)
58: 		}
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	if err := s.plans.MarkMealConsumed(ctx, mealID); err != nil {
62: 		return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("mark meal consumed: %w", err)
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
66: 
67: 	return dto.ConsumeMealResponse{
68: 		MealID:    mealID,
69: 		Consumed:  true,
70: 		ConsumedAt: now,
71: 		Items:     items,
72: 	}, nil
73: }
74: 
75: func (s *ConsumeService) deductIngredient(ctx context.Context, userID, mealID string, fdcID int64, description string, quantity float64, unit string) dto.ConsumedItemInfo {
76: 	info := dto.ConsumedItemInfo{
77: 		FDCID:           fdcID,
78: 		Description:     description,
79: 		QuantityDeducted: quantity,
80: 		Unit:            unit,
81: 	}
82: 
83: 	deduction, err := s.pantry.DeductByFood(ctx, userID, fdcID, unit, quantity)
84: 	if errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) {
85: 		info.BeforeQuantity = 0
86: 		info.AfterQuantity = 0
87: 		info.QuantityDeducted = 0
88: 		info.Warning = fmt.Sprintf("no pantry item found for %s (%s)", description, unit)
89: 
90: 		_ = s.consumption.Insert(ctx, repositories.ConsumptionLogEntry{
91: 			PlanMealID:       mealID,
92: 			UserID:           userID,
93: 			FDCID:            fdcID,
94: 			QuantityDeducted: 0,
95: 			Unit:             unit,
96: 			Warning:          info.Warning,
97: 		})
98: 		return info
99: 	}
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		info.Warning = fmt.Sprintf("failed to deduct: %v", err)
102: 		return info
103: 	}
104: 
105: 	info.PantryItemID = deduction.PantryItemID
106: 	info.BeforeQuantity = deduction.BeforeQty
107: 	info.AfterQuantity = deduction.AfterQty
108: 
109: 	if deduction.BeforeQty < quantity {
110: 		info.Warning = fmt.Sprintf("insufficient quantity: had %.2f %s, needed %.2f %s", deduction.BeforeQty, unit, quantity, unit)
111: 		info.QuantityDeducted = deduction.BeforeQty - deduction.AfterQty
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	_ = s.consumption.Insert(ctx, repositories.ConsumptionLogEntry{
115: 		PlanMealID:       mealID,
116: 		UserID:           userID,
117: 		FDCID:            fdcID,
118: 		PantryItemID:     deduction.PantryItemID,
119: 		QuantityDeducted: info.QuantityDeducted,
120: 		Unit:             unit,
121: 		BeforeQuantity:   deduction.BeforeQty,
122: 		AfterQuantity:    deduction.AfterQty,
123: 		Warning:          info.Warning,
124: 	})
125: 
126: 	return info
127: }

29: 		return
30: 	}
31: 
32: 	res, err := h.consume.ConsumeMeal(r.Context(), userID, mealID)
33: 	if err != nil {
34: 		switch {
35: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrPlanMealNotFound):
36: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusNotFound, "not_found", err.Error())
37: 		case errors.Is(err, services.ErrMealAlreadyConsumed):
38: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusConflict, "conflict", err.Error())
39: 		default:
40: 			WriteAPIError(w, http.StatusInternalServerError, "internal_error", "failed to consume meal")
41: 		}
42: 		return
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	WriteJSON(w, http.StatusOK, res)
46: }

148: func (r *PantryRepository) DeductByFood(ctx context.Context, userID string, fdcID int64, unit string, quantity float64) (PantryDeduction, error) {
149: 	var d PantryDeduction
150: 	err := r.db.QueryRowContext(
151: 		ctx,
152: 		`SELECT p.id, p.quantity, f.description
153: 		 FROM pantry_items p
154: 		 JOIN usda_foods f ON f.fdc_id = p.fdc_id
155: 		 WHERE p.user_id = ? AND p.fdc_id = ? AND p.unit = ?`,
156: 		userID,
157: 		fdcID,
158: 		unit,
159: 	).Scan(&d.PantryItemID, &d.BeforeQty, &d.FoodDesc)
160: 	if err != nil {
161: 		return PantryDeduction{}, err
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	newQty := d.BeforeQty - quantity
165: 	if newQty < 0 {
166: 		newQty = 0
167: 	}
168: 
169: 	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
170: 		ctx,
171: 		`UPDATE pantry_items SET quantity = ?, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = ?`,
172: 		newQty,
173: 		d.PantryItemID,
174: 	)
175: 	if err != nil {
176: 		return PantryDeduction{}, err
177: 	}
178: 
179: 	d.AfterQty = newQty
180: 	return d, nil
181: }

All checks pass. Let me provide a clean summary.

Wait, let me double-check one thing: the `DeductByFood` joins on `p.fdc_id = ? AND p.unit = ?`. The `consume_service.go` deductIngredient passes `ing.FDCID` and `ing.Unit`. What does the `ConsumptionLogEntry` BeforeQuantity default to? In `consumption_log_repository.go`, the struct definition has `BeforeQuantity float64` and `AfterQuantity float64` — they'll both default to 0.0 if not explicitly set.

But in the no-match case (pantry item not found), the code at line 83-98 does:
- info.BeforeQuantity = 0
- info.AfterQuantity = 0
- info.QuantityDeducted = 0
- insert entry with BeforeQuantity: 0, AfterQuantity: 0 (since they're zero-valued in the struct)

That looks right — the struct fields BeforeQuantity and AfterQuantity will be 0 after initialization, so the Insert call will correctly log 0 for both.

One issue I notice: In the `ConsumptionLogEntry`, `BeforeQuantity` and `AfterQuantity` default to 0. When there's no match (pantry item not found), the code does:
```go
_ = s.consumption.Insert(ctx, repositories.ConsumptionLogEntry{
    PlanMealID:       mealID,
    UserID:           userID,
    FDCID:            fdcID,
    QuantityDeducted: 0,
    Unit:             unit,
    Warning:          info.Warning,
})
```
Since `BeforeQuantity` and `AfterQuantity` are not set here, they're 0. That's correct — there was no pantry item.

One more thing: the consumption_log schema defines `before_quantity REAL NOT NULL` and `after_quantity REAL NOT NULL` with CHECK (>= 0). Setting to 0 for the not-found case is fine.

Everything checks out for Step 1. Let me update the todo list.

11: type ChatRepository struct {

15: func NewChatRepository(db *sql.DB) *ChatRepository {
16: 	return &ChatRepository{db: db}
17: }
18: 
19: type StoredChatMessage struct {
20: 	ID        string
21: 	UserID    string
22: 	Role      string
23: 	Action    string
24: 	Content   string
25: 	CreatedAt string
26: }
27: 
28: func (r *ChatRepository) InsertUserMessage(ctx context.Context, userID, message, action string) (StoredChatMessage, error) {
29: 	msgID, err := id.New("msg")
30: 	if err != nil {
31: 		return StoredChatMessage{}, err
32: 	}
33: 
34: 	now := time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)
35: 
36: 	_, err = r.db.ExecContext(
37: 		ctx,
38: 		`INSERT INTO chat_messages (id, user_id, role, action, content, metadata_json, created_at)
39: 		 VALUES (?, ?, 'user', NULLIF(?, ''), ?, '{}', ?)`,
40: 		msgID,
41: 		userID,
42: 		action,
43: 		message,
44: 		now,
45: 	)
46: 	if err != nil {
47: 		return StoredChatMessage{}, err
48: 	}
49: 
50: 	return StoredChatMessage{
51: 		ID:        msgID,
52: 		UserID:    userID,
53: 		Role:      "user",
54: 		Action:    action,
55: 		Content:   message,
56: 		CreatedAt: now,
57: 	}, nil
58: }
59: 
60: func (r *ChatRepository) ListRecent(ctx context.Context, userID string, limit int) ([]StoredChatMessage, error) {
61: 	if limit <= 0 || limit > 100 {
62: 		limit = 50
63: 	}
64: 
65: 	rows, err := r.db.QueryContext(
66: 		ctx,
67: 		`SELECT id, user_id, role, COALESCE(action, ''), content, created_at
68: 		 FROM chat_messages
69: 		 WHERE user_id = ?
70: 		 ORDER BY created_at DESC
71: 		 LIMIT ?`,
72: 		userID,
73: 		limit,
74: 	)
75: 	if err != nil {
76: 		return nil, err
77: 	}
78: 	defer rows.Close()
79: 
80: 	messages := make([]StoredChatMessage, 0, limit)
81: 	for rows.Next() {
82: 		var msg StoredChatMessage
83: 		if err := rows.Scan(&msg.ID, &msg.UserID, &msg.Role, &msg.Action, &msg.Content, &msg.CreatedAt); err != nil {
84: 			return nil, err
85: 		}
86: 		messages = append(messages, msg)
87: 	}
88: 	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
89: 		return nil, err
90: 	}
91: 
92: 	for i, j := 0, len(messages)-1; i < j; i, j = i+1, j-1 {
93: 		messages[i], messages[j] = messages[j], messages[i]
94: 	}
95: 
96: 	return messages, nil
97: }

183: 		response, err := c.doRequest(ctx, endpoint, body)
184: 		if err == nil {

20: 	if err != nil {
21: 		return dto.ChatMessageResponse{}, err
22: 	}
23: 	return dto.ChatMessageResponse{
24: 		ID:        msg.ID,
25: 		Role:      msg.Role,
26: 		Action:    msg.Action,
27: 		Content:   msg.Content,
28: 		CreatedAt: msg.CreatedAt,
29: 	}, nil
30: }
31: 
32: func (s *ChatService) GetHistory(ctx context.Context, userID string, limit int) (dto.ChatHistoryResponse, error) {
33: 	messages, err := s.chat.ListRecent(ctx, userID, limit)
34: 	if err != nil {
35: 		return dto.ChatHistoryResponse{}, err
36: 	}
37: 	out := make([]dto.ChatMessageResponse, 0, len(messages))
38: 	for _, msg := range messages {
39: 		out = append(out, dto.ChatMessageResponse{
40: 			ID:        msg.ID,
41: 			Role:      msg.Role,
42: 			Action:    msg.Action,
43: 			Content:   msg.Content,
44: 			CreatedAt: msg.CreatedAt,
45: 		})
46: 	}
47: 	return dto.ChatHistoryResponse{Messages: out}, nil
48: }

9: func FallbackWeekPlan(startDate string) (NormalizedPlan, error) {
10: 	start, err := time.Parse("2006-01-02", startDate)
11: 	if err != nil {
12: 		start = time.Now().UTC().Truncate(24 * time.Hour)
13: 	}
14: 	end := start.AddDate(0, 0, 6)
15: 
16: 	rotation := buildRotation()
17: 
18: 	totalCost := 0.0
19: 	days := make([]DayPlan, 7)
20: 	for i := 0; i < 7; i++ {
21: 		date := start.AddDate(0, 0, i).Format("2006-01-02")
22: 		dayMeals := rotation[i%len(rotation)]
23: 		meals := make([]MealDetail, 4)
24: 		copy(meals, dayMeals)
25: 		for mi := range meals {
26: 			totalCost += meals[mi].EstimatedCost
27: 		}
28: 		days[i] = DayPlan{Date: date, Meals: meals}
29: 	}
30: 
31: 	payload := PlanResponse{
32: 		PeriodType:     "week",
33: 		StartDate:      start.Format("2006-01-02"),
34: 		EndDate:        end.Format("2006-01-02"),
35: 		EstimatedTotal: totalCost,
36: 		Days:           days,
37: 		Notes:          "Fallback plan generated from seeded recipes",
38: 	}
39: 
40: 	raw, err := json.Marshal(payload)
41: 	if err != nil {
42: 		return NormalizedPlan{}, fmt.Errorf("fallback marshal: %w", err)
43: 	}
44: 
45: 	normalized, err := ParsePlanResponse(string(raw))

49: 
50: 	normalized.Proposal.Source = "fallback"
51: 	return normalized, nil
52: }

106: 	if parsed1.Proposal.AICostCentsTotal <= 0 || parsed2.Proposal.AICostCentsTotal <= 0 {
107: 		t.Fatal("both plans should have positive total cost")
108: 	}
109: }
